description: The anticipated date on which the replenishment order is expected to arrive at the destination node, calculated from order_date plus lead_time_days. Used for inventory availability planning and receiving dock scheduling.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning Lead Time Calculation; NRF Supply Chain SLA Standards

description: The physical supply chain channel through which this replenishment order will be fulfilled. dc_to_store = Distribution Center to store; supplier_to_store = Direct Store Delivery (DSD); supplier_to_dc = inbound to DC; cross_dock = Cross-Docking without DC storage; dsd = Direct Store Delivery; drop_ship = vendor ships directly to end customer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dc_to_store|supplier_to_store|supplier_to_dc|cross_dock|dsd|drop_ship — promote to reference product]
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Fulfillment Channels; NRF Omnichannel Fulfillment Standards

description: Indicates whether this replenishment order was flagged as an emergency order due to an imminent or active out-of-stock situation. Emergency orders bypass standard lead time and approval workflows. True = emergency; False = standard.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning Emergency Replenishment; NRF Out-of-Stock Management

description: Indicates whether this replenishment order is managed under a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) agreement where the supplier is responsible for maintaining stock levels at the destination node. True = VMI order; False = retailer-managed replenishment.
reference: NRF Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM Consignment and VMI

description: The Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) stipulated by the supplier for this SKU at the time the replenishment order was created. Used to validate that ordered_quantity meets supplier contractual requirements. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM purchasing info record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing Info Record; NRF Supplier Management Standards

description: Indicates whether the ordered_quantity meets or exceeds the supplier's Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) requirement. True = compliant; False = below MOQ threshold, requiring buyer review or override.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MOQ Validation; NRF Procurement Best Practices

description: The principal business event timestamp — the date and time the replenishment order was created and submitted by the demand planning or replenishment system (Blue Yonder or SAP MM). Distinct from audit timestamps.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning Order Creation; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Externally-known business identifier for the replenishment order, sourced from Blue Yonder Demand Planning or SAP S/4HANA MM module. Used for cross-system traceability and supplier communication via EDI.
reference: GS1 EDI Standards; SAP MM Purchase Order Numbering

description: Current lifecycle state of the replenishment order. Tracks progression from draft creation through approval, shipment, receipt at destination node, and closure. Cancelled indicates the order was voided before fulfillment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|in_transit|received|partially_received|cancelled|closed — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchase Order Status; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) Order Lifecycle

description: Classification of how the replenishment order was triggered. Auto-replenishment is system-generated based on reorder policy thresholds; manual is buyer-initiated; VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory) is supplier-driven; emergency covers urgent out-of-stock situations; cross_dock bypasses DC storage; drop_ship routes directly from vendor to destination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: auto_replenishment|manual|vmi|emergency|cross_dock|drop_ship — promote to reference product]
reference: NRF Replenishment Best Practices; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) Order Type Classification

description: The quantity of the SKU originally requested in the replenishment order, expressed in the SKU's base unit of measure (e.g., each, case, pallet). This is the raw demand signal before supplier approval or MOQ adjustment.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) Order Quantity; NRF Inventory Management Standards

description: Business priority assigned to this replenishment order, used to sequence fulfillment and allocate DC resources. Critical = imminent out-of-stock risk; high = elevated demand or promotional support; standard = routine replenishment; low = opportunistic or pre-build.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning Priority Scoring; NRF Inventory Management Best Practices

description: The actual quantity of the SKU physically received and confirmed at the destination node. Used to calculate fill rate and identify short shipments. Populated upon goods receipt in Manhattan Associates WMS or SAP S/4HANA WM.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Goods Receipt; SAP S/4HANA WM; NRF Fill Rate KPI

description: The operational system of record that originated this replenishment order. blue_yonder = Blue Yonder Demand Planning auto-replenishment; sap_mm = SAP S/4HANA MM manual purchase order; orms = Oracle Retail Merchandising System; manual = buyer-entered; vmi_portal = supplier VMI portal submission.
reference: Informatica MDM Data Lineage; ISO/IEC 27001 Data Provenance

description: The native order identifier from the originating source system (e.g., SAP MM document number, Blue Yonder order ID, ORMS order number). Enables reverse lookup and reconciliation with the system of record.
reference: Informatica MDM Cross-Reference; SAP S/4HANA MM Document Number

description: The total cost of the replenishment order calculated as approved_quantity multiplied by unit_cost. Represents the financial commitment for this replenishment event. Used for Open to Buy (OTB) budget tracking and P&L reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 330 — Inventory Valuation; FASB ASC 606 Revenue Recognition; NRF OTB Management

description: The mechanism that triggered this replenishment order. reorder_point = on-hand fell below reorder point threshold; min_max = inventory fell below minimum level; days_of_supply = Weeks of Supply (WOS) fell below threshold; manual_override = buyer-initiated; vmi_signal = Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) supplier signal; promotional = triggered by upcoming promotion event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reorder_point|min_max|days_of_supply|manual_override|vmi_signal|promotional — promote to reference product]
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning Replenishment Triggers; NRF Inventory Replenishment Best Practices

description: The Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) per unit for this SKU at the time of the replenishment order, sourced from the SAP S/4HANA MM purchasing info record or Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS). Used for inventory valuation and GMROI analysis.
reference: FASB ASC 330 — Inventory Valuation; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO COGS; NRF GMROI Standards

description: Audit timestamp recording when this replenishment order record was last modified in the Silver Layer data platform. Supports change detection and incremental processing.
Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each inter-node stock transfer record in the inventory management system.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module

description: Reference to the logistics carrier or 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) provider responsible for transporting the stock transfer between nodes. Used for carrier performance analytics and SLA compliance reporting.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA Retail SD Module

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Transfer operations incur labor and freight costs allocated to cost centers for P&L tracking. Stock_transfer needs cost_center_id to charge transfer costs to responsible departments and measure transf

Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Transfer accounting and intercompany pricing require cost data for inventory valuation during movement. Store-to-store transfers, DC-to-store shipments, and cross-dock operations need cost basis for f
Identifier of the receiving inventory node (store, DC, MFC, or vendor location) to which the stock transfer is directed. Used for inventory receipt confirmation and network replenishment tracking.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA Retail WM Module

description: Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Ship-from-store fulfillment: a fulfillment order for ship-from-store triggers a stock transfer between the store inventory node and the outbound staging area. Linking stock_transfer to fulfillment_ord

Foreign key linking to supplychain.inventory_transfer. Business justification: inventory.stock_transfer and supplychain.inventory_transfer represent the same business concept from different system layers (IMS vs. SCM execution). Linking them enables cross-system transfer quantit

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Inter-node stock transfers in retail generate journal entries for inter-company or inter-cost-center inventory transfers. Finance requires traceability from stock_transfer to its GL posting for period

Foreign key linking to supplychain.outbound_shipment. Business justification: A stock_transfer (DC-to-store or store-to-store) is physically executed as an outbound_shipment from the DC. Linking stock_transfer to outbound_shipment enables transfer-in-transit tracking and carrie
Identifier of the originating inventory node (store, DC, MFC, or vendor location) from which the stock transfer is dispatched. Supports network balancing and omnichannel fulfillment analytics.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA Retail WM Module

description: Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Promotional inventory redistribution tracking: retailers execute inter-node stock transfers to position inventory at high-traffic stores ahead of campaign launches. Direct campaign linkage on stock_tr

Reference to the originating purchase order that triggered this stock transfer, applicable for DC-to-store replenishment flows sourced from supplier inbound receipts. Links transfer to procurement context.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS)

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.replenishment_order. Business justification: Stock transfers between nodes are frequently triggered by replenishment orders — when a store needs restocking from a DC, a replenishment order is raised and a stock transfer is initiated. Linking sto

Reference to the replenishment plan that generated this stock transfer, linking the physical movement to the demand-driven planning signal from Blue Yonder or Oracle Retail. Supports replenishment accuracy analytics.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS)

description: Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) being transferred. The SKU is the atomic unit of inventory tracking, uniquely identifying a product variant (size, color, style) within the retail assortment.
reference: GS1 Global Product Classification; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); Informatica MDM

description: Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Inter-node stock transfers require UOM master reference to ensure transfer_quantity is expressed in the correct unit for both source and destination nodes. Retail DC-to-store and store-to-store transf

Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: stock_transfer has is_vendor_managed flag but no vendor reference. VMI replenishment transfers must be attributed to the managing vendor for chargeback processing, VMI settlement, and vendor complianc

Calendar date on which the stock transfer was physically received and confirmed at the destination node. Compared against expected_receipt_date to measure carrier and DC performance against SLA targets.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA Retail WM Module

description: System audit timestamp recording when this stock transfer record was first persisted in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: ISO 27001 Information Security; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the transfer cost is denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency retail operations across international markets.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; FASB ASC 830 Foreign Currency

description: Classification of the receiving inventory node. STORE: retail store; DC: distribution center; MFC: micro-fulfillment center; VENDOR: supplier location for RTV; DARK_STORE: fulfillment-only location.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; NRF Omnichannel Standards

description: Planned calendar date by which the stock transfer is expected to arrive at the destination node. Used for replenishment planning, weeks of supply (WOS) calculations, and SLA compliance monitoring.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning; Manhattan Associates WMS; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS)

description: Date and time when the stock transfer record was first created in the system, representing the business event of transfer initiation. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the transfer lifecycle.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module; Manhattan Associates WMS

business_glossary_term: Inventory Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Per Unit
description: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) value per unit for the transferred SKU at time of transfer, used for inventory valuation, GMROI calculations, and financial reporting. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO standard cost or moving average cost.
reference: FASB ASC 330 Inventory Accounting; SAP S/4HANA Retail FI/CO Module

description: Flag indicating whether this stock transfer is executed as a cross-docking (cross-dock) movement, where inventory is transferred directly from inbound to outbound at the DC without putaway storage. Supports DC throughput and flow-through analytics.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; NRF Supply Chain Standards

description: Flag indicating whether this stock transfer is part of a ship-from-store (SFS) omnichannel fulfillment flow, where a retail store acts as a fulfillment node dispatching inventory to satisfy an e-commerce or BOPIS order.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud; NRF Omnichannel Standards

description: Flag indicating whether this stock transfer is part of a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) program, where the supplier initiates and manages the replenishment transfer rather than the retailer's internal planning team.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); GS1 VMI Standards

description: Free-text operational notes or comments associated with the stock transfer, capturing exception details, special handling instructions, or carrier communication relevant to the transfer execution.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module

description: Operational priority assigned to the stock transfer, influencing pick sequencing, carrier selection, and dispatch scheduling at the DC or store. URGENT transfers may be triggered by stockout alerts or product recalls.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Actual number of units confirmed received at the destination node upon delivery. May differ from transfer_quantity due to transit damage, shrinkage, or carrier discrepancies. Drives inventory reconciliation and shrinkage monitoring.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA Retail WM Module; FASB ASC 330 Inventory Accounting

description: Flag indicating whether this stock transfer was tracked using RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) technology for real-time inventory visibility during transit. Supports RFID-based inventory accuracy programs.
reference: GS1 RFID Standards; NRF RFID Implementation Guidelines

description: Calendar date on which the stock transfer was physically dispatched from the source node. Used for lead time calculation, carrier SLA tracking, and inventory in-transit reporting.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module; GS1 Supply Chain Standards

description: Classification of the originating inventory node. STORE: retail store; DC: distribution center; MFC: micro-fulfillment center; VENDOR: supplier location for RTV or DSD; DARK_STORE: fulfillment-only location.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; NRF Omnichannel Standards

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this stock transfer record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking across the retail technology stack.
reference: ISO 27001 Information Security; Informatica MDM Data Lineage Standards

description: Carrier-assigned shipment tracking number for the stock transfer, enabling real-time in-transit visibility and last-mile delivery monitoring. Used for exception management and carrier SLA validation.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; GS1 Supply Chain Standards

description: Total logistics cost incurred for executing this stock transfer, including carrier freight charges, handling fees, and any 3PL surcharges. Used for inventory valuation (COGS), P&L allocation, and supply chain cost optimization.
reference: FASB ASC 330 Inventory Accounting; SAP S/4HANA Retail FI/CO Module

description: Externally-known business reference number for the stock transfer, used for tracking and communication across supply chain partners, carriers, and store operations. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM transfer order numbering.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module; GS1 Supply Chain Standards

description: Number of units of the SKU authorized and dispatched in this stock transfer. Represents the planned transfer quantity as recorded at time of transfer creation.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module

description: Business reason code explaining why the stock transfer was initiated. REPLENISHMENT: routine inventory restocking; NETWORK_BALANCE: lateral balancing to address overstock/understock; RTV: return to vendor; RECALL: product safety recall per CPSC; DAMAGE_RETURN: return of damaged goods; OMNICHANNEL: ship-from-store or BOPIS fulfillment support.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); CPSC Product Recall Standards; NRF Inventory Management Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the stock transfer. DRAFT: initiated but not yet dispatched; IN_TRANSIT: shipment dispatched from source node; RECEIVED: confirmed receipt at destination node; CANCELLED: transfer voided before completion; EXCEPTION: discrepancy or damage reported during transit.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA Retail WM Module

description: Classification of the inter-node inventory movement. DC_TO_STORE: distribution center replenishment to store; STORE_TO_STORE: lateral balancing between stores; STORE_TO_DC: reverse logistics or consolidation; CROSS_DOCK: direct transfer without storage per cross-docking (DSD) model; RTV: Return to Vendor; MFC_TO_STORE: micro-fulfillment center to store for omnichannel fulfillment (ship-from-store).
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); Manhattan Associates WMS; NRF Supply Chain Standards

description: 12-digit Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode identifier for the transferred SKU, used for scanning and verification during pick, pack, and receipt operations at DC and store nodes.
reference: GS1 US UPC Standards; CPSC Product Identification Requirements

description: System audit timestamp recording the most recent modification to this stock transfer record, such as status updates, quantity adjustments, or receipt confirmations. Supports change data capture and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 27001 Information Security; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: Difference between transfer_quantity and received_quantity, representing units lost, damaged, or unaccounted for during transit. Positive value indicates shortage; negative indicates overage. Drives shrinkage monitoring and carrier chargeback processes.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; FASB ASC 330 Inventory Accounting; NRF Shrinkage Standards

description: Reason code explaining the cause of any quantity variance between dispatched and received units. Used for shrinkage root cause analysis, carrier chargeback (RMA) processing, and inventory reconciliation.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; NRF Shrinkage and Loss Prevention Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for every physical or logical inventory-holding location in the retail network (stores, DCs, MFCs, dark stores, vendor-managed locations, in-transit nodes). Primary key of this master record.

Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_zone. Business justification: Inventory nodes are assigned to cost zones that determine freight, duty, and overhead factors for landed cost calculations on replenishment. cost_zone drives cost_price computation for goods received

Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: An inventory_node of type DC corresponds to a dc_facility record in the supply chain domain. Linking them enables cross-system node-to-facility mapping for WMS integration and capacity planning — a fu

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_node. Business justification: BOPIS/ship-from-store node mapping: retail operations require mapping each inventory node to its corresponding fulfillment node to route BOPIS and SFS orders to the correct fulfillment operation. This

Foreign key linking to store.location. Business justification: Inventory nodes that represent physical stores must be linked to the store master record for store-level inventory dashboards, format-based replenishment rules, and store operations reporting. Retail

Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Every retail store/DC belongs to a price zone that governs which retail prices apply at that location. This is foundational retail pricing architecture — replenishment, stock valuation, and markdown d
Primary street address of the inventory node facility. Used for carrier routing, last-mile delivery coordination, and regulatory filings.
reference: OSHA — Facility Registration; GS1 GLN Address Standard

description: Secondary address detail (suite, dock number, building identifier) for the inventory node facility.
City in which the inventory node is located. Used for geographic reporting, tax jurisdiction assignment, and carrier zone mapping.
reference: GS1 GLN Address Standard; FTC — Pricing Transparency (location-based)

description: Calendar date on which the inventory node was permanently closed or decommissioned. Null for active nodes. Used for historical inventory reporting and asset disposal tracking.
reference: FASB ASC 842 — Lease Termination; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the node location. Governs applicable regulatory frameworks (FDA, CPSC, GDPR/CCPA), currency, and customs rules.
Timestamp when this inventory node master record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports MDM audit trails and data lineage in Informatica MDM.
reference: ISO 27001 — Information Security; Informatica MDM — Record Audit

description: Scheduled frequency at which physical inventory cycle counts are conducted at this node. Drives WMS task scheduling and shrinkage monitoring cadence.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS — Cycle Count Scheduling; NRF Inventory Accuracy Standards

description: Internal district code within a region, used for field management span-of-control, district-level KPI reporting, and workforce scheduling via Workday HCM.
reference: NRF — Retail Organizational Hierarchy; Workday HCM — Cost Center

description: Number of inbound/outbound dock doors at the node. Determines receiving throughput capacity, inbound appointment scheduling windows, and cross-docking feasibility in Manhattan Associates WMS.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS — Facility Configuration; NRF Supply Chain Standards

description: Retail format identifier assigned to the node (e.g., 'HYPERMARKET', 'DISCOUNT', 'DEPT_STORE', 'ECOMM_FC'). Drives assortment breadth/depth rules in ORMS and planogram (POG) assignments.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) — Location Format; NRF

description: GS1-issued 13-digit Global Location Number uniquely identifying this node in the global supply chain. Used in EDI transactions (ASC X12 / EDIFACT), RFID-based receiving, and supplier communications.
reference: GS1 — Global Location Number (GLN) Standard; EDI ASC X12

business_glossary_term: Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether the node supports Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) fulfillment. Drives order routing logic in the OMS and Salesforce Commerce Cloud checkout flow.
reference: NRF — Omnichannel Fulfillment Standards; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Indicates whether this node acts as a drop-ship origin point where a vendor ships directly to the end customer on behalf of the retailer, bypassing DC inventory.
reference: NRF — Drop Ship Program Standards; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Indicates whether the node is equipped with RFID infrastructure for real-time inventory tracking, cycle count automation, and shrinkage reduction.
reference: GS1 — RFID/EPC Standards; NRF RFID in Retail Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Reserve Online Pick Up In Store (ROPIS) Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether the node supports Reserve Online Pick Up In Store (ROPIS), allowing customers to reserve items online and collect without pre-payment.
reference: NRF — Omnichannel Fulfillment Standards; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Indicates whether the node is configured as a Ship-from-Store (SFS) fulfillment center, allowing it to pick, pack, and ship e-commerce orders directly to customers.
reference: NRF — Omnichannel Fulfillment Standards; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Indicates whether this node participates in a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) program where the supplier is responsible for maintaining stock levels.
reference: NRF — VMI Program Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM — Consignment

description: Date of the most recent physical inventory cycle count performed at this node. Used to assess inventory accuracy, schedule next count, and monitor shrinkage compliance.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS — Cycle Count Management; NRF Inventory Accuracy Standards

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this inventory node master record. Used for change data capture (CDC), MDM synchronization, and Silver Layer incremental processing in Databricks.
reference: ISO 27001 — Information Security; Informatica MDM — Record Audit

description: WGS-84 decimal latitude coordinate of the node. Enables geospatial proximity analysis for BOPIS radius calculations, last-mile routing optimization, and store clustering.
reference: ISO 6709 — Geographic Coordinates; GS1 GLN Geolocation

description: WGS-84 decimal longitude coordinate of the node. Used alongside latitude for geospatial routing, catchment area analysis, and omnichannel fulfillment optimization.
reference: ISO 6709 — Geographic Coordinates; GS1 GLN Geolocation

description: Name of the store manager, DC operations manager, or facility lead responsible for this inventory node. Used for escalation routing, audit accountability, and Workday HCM workforce reporting.
reference: Workday HCM — Organizational Hierarchy; OSHA — Facility Responsibility

description: Maximum number of distinct SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) the node can actively stock based on shelf slots, bin locations, and planogram (POG) configuration. Used in assortment planning and OTB (Open to Buy) calculations.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) — Assortment Planning; NRF

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the node as maintained in SAP S/4HANA WM and Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS). Used in EDI transactions, purchase orders, and replenishment plans to reference the location. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER (MASTER_RESOURCE canonical category).
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM — Storage Location; GS1 GLN Standard

description: Human-readable display name of the inventory node (e.g., 'Chicago Midway DC', 'Store #0421 – Oak Park'). Used in operational reports, planograms, and WMS screens. Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL (MASTER_RESOURCE canonical category).

Categorical classification of the inventory-holding location. Drives fulfillment capability rules, replenishment logic, and reporting hierarchies. Valid values: store (retail selling floor), distribution_center (DC), micro_fulfillment_center (MFC), dark_store (fulfillment-only), vendor_managed (VMI location), in_transit (goods in movement). Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE (MASTER_RESOURCE canonical category).
reference: NRF — Omnichannel Fulfillment Taxonomy; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Calendar date on which the inventory node became operationally active and began receiving or fulfilling inventory. Used for comp sales (SSS) eligibility determination and new-store ramp analysis.
reference: NRF — Comparable Store Sales (SSS) Definition; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Current lifecycle state of the inventory node. Controls whether the node is eligible to receive stock, fulfill orders, or appear in replenishment plans. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS (MASTER_RESOURCE canonical category).
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM — Storage Location Status; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Legal ownership or operating arrangement for the node facility. Drives lease accounting under FASB ASC 842, asset capitalization rules, and P&L cost allocation.
reference: FASB ASC 842 — Lease Accounting; SAP S/4HANA FI — Asset Management

description: Primary operational phone number for the inventory node facility. Used for carrier coordination, inbound appointment confirmation, and emergency contact.
Postal or ZIP code of the inventory node. Used for carrier zone determination, last-mile delivery routing, and geographic market analysis.
reference: GS1 GLN Address Standard; USPS ZIP Code Standard

description: Node code of the primary Distribution Center (DC) that replenishes this node. Used in replenishment routing, inbound appointment scheduling, and supply chain network optimization.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS — Replenishment Routing; Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Internal geographic region code grouping nodes for regional P&L reporting, district management, and comp sales (SSS) analysis (e.g., 'MIDWEST', 'NORTHEAST', 'WEST_COAST').
reference: NRF — Retail Geographic Hierarchy; SAP S/4HANA CO — Profit Center

description: Standard number of days from replenishment order placement to stock receipt at this node. Used by Blue Yonder Demand Planning to calculate reorder points, weeks of supply (WOS), and safety stock levels.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning — Lead Time Parameter; NRF Supply Chain Standards

description: Minimum days of supply maintained as safety stock buffer at this node to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. Feeds Blue Yonder replenishment planning and WOS calculations.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning — Safety Stock; NRF Inventory Management Standards

description: Net selling floor area in square feet, excluding back-of-house, receiving docks, and storage. Used for sales-per-square-foot KPI, planogram space allocation, and GMROI analysis.
reference: NRF — Retail Space Productivity; FASB ASC 842

description: ISO 3166-2 state or province code for the node location. Drives sales tax rules, labor law compliance, and regional reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-2; FASB ASC 606 — Revenue Recognition by Jurisdiction

description: Secondary classification providing finer granularity within node_type (e.g., hypermarket, department_store, discount_outlet, cross_dock_dc, ambient_dc, cold_chain_dc, ship_from_store_hub). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hypermarket|department_store|discount_outlet|cross_dock_dc|ambient_dc|cold_chain_dc|ship_from_store_hub|ecommerce_fc — promote to reference product]
reference: NRF — Store Format Classification; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Storage temperature classification for the node. Governs which SKU categories (grocery, frozen, pharma) can be stocked and drives cold-chain compliance requirements under FDA food safety regulations.
reference: FDA — Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); ISO 22000 — Food Safety Management

description: IANA time zone identifier for the node (e.g., 'America/Chicago'). Critical for accurate timestamp normalization of POS transactions, cycle count scheduling, and SLA window calculations across multi-timezone networks.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; SAP S/4HANA — Plant/Storage Location Time Zone

description: Total physical storage area of the node measured in square feet. Used for capacity planning, space utilization analysis, and GMROI calculations. Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE (MASTER_RESOURCE canonical category).
reference: NRF — Retail Space Productivity Standards; FASB ASC 842 (lease area reporting)

description: Unique identifier for the inventory lot or batch record. Primary key for the lot entity.

Foreign key linking to merchandising.category. Business justification: Product recall and quality issue management requires category-level aggregation for buyer notification and regulatory reporting. Lot traceability reporting is organized by merchandise category. Catego

Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Lot-level costing for FEFO/FIFO valuation and batch-specific cost tracking. Perishables, pharmaceuticals, and regulated products require lot-specific cost basis for accurate inventory valuation, recal
Reference to the goods receipt transaction that brought this lot into inventory. Links to receiving documentation.

Foreign key linking to supplychain.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Lot traceability requires linking each lot to the inbound shipment that delivered it. This supports product recall management, FEFO rotation, and food safety regulatory compliance — a mandatory retail

Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_node. Business justification: Lots are physically stored at specific inventory nodes (stores, DCs, MFCs). The lot master currently has storage_location_code (STRING) as a denormalized bin-level reference, but lacks a direct FK to
Self-referencing FK on lot (parent_lot_id)
Reference to the purchase order that sourced this lot. Links lot traceability to procurement records.

Foreign key linking to supplychain.receiving_event. Business justification: Lot creation is triggered by the receiving_event (quality inspection results map directly to lot quality_status). Linking lot to receiving_event enables lot-level quality audit trails and recall trace
Reference to the product SKU that this lot represents. Links lot-level tracking to product master data.

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Each lot contributes to a specific stock position (SKU+node combination). The lot tracks individual batch quantities (available_quantity, quantity) that roll up into the aggregate stock_position. Link

Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Lot-level quantity tracking for perishables (FEFO sequencing, expiry management) requires UOM master reference. Retail food safety and FSMA compliance reporting requires consistent UOM for lot availab
Reference to the supplier or vendor who provided this lot. Critical for recall management and vendor performance tracking.

Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor_item. Business justification: Lot traceability for food safety, recall management, and regulatory compliance requires linking each lot to the vendor's item specification (country_of_origin, GTIN, certification). Retail food safety

Foreign key linking to supplychain.warehouse_zone. Business justification: Lots are stored in specific warehouse zones (temperature-controlled, hazmat, ambient). Linking lot to warehouse_zone enables FEFO-compliant putaway zone assignment and temperature compliance tracking
Current quantity available for sale or allocation from this lot. Decreases as units are sold or reserved.
Manufacturer or supplier assigned batch identifier. May differ from internal lot number for vendor-managed inventory scenarios.
Date indicating optimal quality period. Product may still be safe after this date but quality may degrade. Distinct from expiry date.
Product certification or compliance code for the lot. Examples: organic, kosher, halal, fair trade, non-GMO. Supports labeling and marketing claims.
Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the product was manufactured or grown. Required for customs and labeling compliance.
Timestamp when the lot record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance reporting.
Three-letter ISO currency code for unit cost. Standard codes include USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, etc.
Date when the product lot expires and should no longer be sold or consumed. Mandatory for perishable goods and pharmaceuticals.
Sequence number for FEFO picking logic. Lower numbers indicate earlier expiry and should be picked first to minimize waste.
Global Trade Item Number for the product. May be UPC, EAN, or other GTIN format. Used for cross-enterprise traceability.
Date when quality inspection was performed on the lot. Required for compliance with food safety and product safety regulations.
Detailed outcome or notes from the quality inspection process. Captures defects, non-conformances, or approval notes.
Indicates whether this lot is a private label or store brand product. True if private label; false if national brand.
Indicates whether this lot is subject to a product recall. True if recalled; false otherwise. Triggers immediate hold and customer notification.
Indicates whether this lot is part of a vendor-managed inventory agreement. True if VMI; false if retailer-owned.
Business identifier for the lot or batch. Human-readable code used for tracking and traceability across the supply chain.

Current lifecycle status of the lot. Determines whether the lot is available for sale, held for quality inspection, or restricted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: available|quarantine|hold|released|expired|recalled|disposed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Date when the product lot was manufactured or produced. Critical for shelf life calculation and food safety compliance.
Free-text notes or comments about the lot. Captures special handling instructions, quality observations, or operational remarks.
Quality inspection outcome for the lot. Approved lots are available for sale; rejected lots must be returned or disposed.
Total quantity of units in the lot at receipt. Used for inventory valuation and traceability reporting.
Official recall case or reference number issued by regulatory authority or manufacturer. Links to recall management system.
Date when the lot was received into inventory at the destination node. Used for aging analysis and FEFO sequencing.
Number of days remaining until expiry as of the current date. Calculated field used for near-expiry alerts and markdown triggers.
Total shelf life of the product in days from manufacture date to expiry date. Used for aging analysis and markdown planning.
Identifier of the operational system that created or owns this lot record. Examples: WMS, ERP, MDM. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
Warehouse bin, zone, or storage location where the lot is physically stored. Supports pick/pack operations and cycle counting.
Supplier's original lot or batch identifier. Used for traceability back to the supplier's manufacturing records.
Required storage temperature zone for the lot. Critical for cold chain compliance and food safety.
Cost per unit for this lot. Used for inventory valuation and cost of goods sold calculation. May vary by lot due to pricing changes.
Timestamp when the lot record was last modified. Tracks changes to lot status, quantity, or other attributes for audit purposes.
Unique surrogate identifier for each stock ledger transaction record. Primary key for the perpetual inventory ledger at the SKU-location-date level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS)

Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_node. Business justification: Every stock ledger transaction occurs at a specific inventory node (store, DC, MFC, dark store). While stock_ledger already has location_id pointing to store.location (cross-domain), it lacks a direct

Foreign key linking to supplychain.inventory_transfer. Business justification: Inter-node inventory transfers generate stock_ledger entries at origin (debit) and destination (credit). Linking stock_ledger to inventory_transfer enables transfer reconciliation and in-transit valua

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Every inventory movement with financial impact (shrinkage, cost adjustments, valuation changes) generates a GL journal entry. Retail finance requires direct traceability from stock_ledger transaction

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Retail companies operating under multiple accounting standards (IFRS/local GAAP) post inventory movements to specific ledgers. Linking stock_ledger to finance.ledger enables parallel valuation reporti
Reference to the inventory node (store, Distribution Center (DC), Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC), or dark store) where the stock movement occurred.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Retail WM Module; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: stock_ledger currently stores lot_number as a denormalized STRING. The lot product is the authoritative master for lot/batch tracking. Adding lot_id as a proper FK normalizes this relationship, enabli

Foreign key linking to merchandising.markdown_event. Business justification: Markdown impact reconciliation: markdown events trigger retail inventory value reductions recorded in the stock ledger. Linking stock_ledger to markdown_event enables actual vs. projected margin impac

Foreign key linking to pricing.markdown. Business justification: Markdown write-downs generate stock ledger entries that reduce the retail book value of inventory. Retail inventory accounting (retail method) requires each markdown-driven ledger entry to reference t

Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Order fulfillment COGS reporting and inventory deduction audit: when an order line is picked/shipped, a stock_ledger entry records the inventory movement. Linking to order_line enables line-level COGS

Foreign key linking to supplychain.outbound_shipment. Business justification: Outbound shipments from DCs to stores generate stock_ledger debit entries. Linking stock_ledger to outbound_shipment enables outbound movement reconciliation and shrinkage detection between pick confi

Foreign key linking to supplychain.receiving_event. Business justification: Receiving events at DCs trigger stock_ledger postings (goods receipt movements). Linking stock_ledger to receiving_event enables cross-system audit trails for discrepancy resolution and financial reco

Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original stock_ledger_id that this record reverses. Populated only when reversal_flag = True. Enables bidirectional audit trail linking between original and reversal entries.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Retail FI/CO Module; FASB ASC 330 Inventory Accounting

description: Foreign key linking to fulfillment.shipment. Business justification: Inventory deduction audit: when goods ship, the stock ledger entry must be traceable to the specific shipment for inventory-to-shipment reconciliation reporting. Retail finance and loss-prevention tea
Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) involved in this stock movement transaction. Links to the product master for item-level inventory tracking.
reference: GS1 Global Product Classification; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); Informatica MDM

description: Foreign key linking to pricing.sku_price. Business justification: Financial reconciliation requires linking inventory movements to pricing effective at transaction time. COGS calculations, margin analysis, and GL posting validation depend on capturing the price-at-t

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Each stock ledger transaction is associated with a specific stock position (SKU+node combination). The stock_ledger is the perpetual movement record that drives the stock_position aggregate. Linking s

Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Financial stock ledger postings (extended_cost, extended_retail_value) depend on correct UOM for quantity-to-value calculations. Linking to product.uom master enforces referential integrity for GL pos
Reference to the supplier associated with this stock movement, applicable for receipt and Direct Store Delivery (DSD) transactions. Null for internal transfers and sales depletions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS)

description: The omnichannel fulfillment channel through which this stock movement was triggered. STORE = in-store POS sale; ECOMMERCE = online order depletion; BOPIS = Buy Online Pick Up In Store; SHIP_FROM_STORE = store-as-fulfillment-center; DC = Distribution Center; MFC = Micro-Fulfillment Center; DARK_STORE = fulfillment-only location. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: STORE|ECOMMERCE|BOPIS|SHIP_FROM_STORE|DC|MFC|DARK_STORE — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: National Retail Federation (NRF) Omnichannel Standards; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: The timestamp when this stock ledger record was first created in the data platform. Represents the system audit creation time, distinct from the business transaction_timestamp. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer processing tracking.
reference: ISO 27001 Information Security Management; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability)

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which unit_cost, extended_cost, unit_retail_price, and extended_retail_value are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency retail operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; FASB ASC 830 Foreign Currency Matters

description: Indicates whether this stock movement is associated with dead stock (unsold, obsolete inventory being written off or liquidated). True = dead stock write-off or clearance movement. Supports inventory health reporting and markdown decision-making.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); National Retail Federation (NRF)

description: Indicates whether this receipt transaction was fulfilled via Direct Store Delivery (DSD), where the supplier delivers directly to the store bypassing the Distribution Center (DC). True = DSD transaction. Impacts supply chain routing and vendor compliance tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); National Retail Federation (NRF)

description: The expiration or best-before date of the inventory units involved in this transaction. Applicable for perishable grocery, pharmaceutical, and health/beauty products. Used for FEFO (First Expired First Out) rotation, dead stock identification, and FDA compliance.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC); GS1 Standards

business_glossary_term: Extended Cost (Extended Cost of Goods Sold - COGS)
description: Total cost value of this stock movement, calculated as movement_quantity multiplied by unit_cost. Represents the financial impact on inventory valuation and feeds the general ledger Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) account. Used for financial reconciliation.
reference: FASB ASC 330 Inventory Accounting; SAP S/4HANA Retail FI/CO Module

description: Total retail value of this stock movement, calculated as movement_quantity multiplied by unit_retail_price. Used in the retail inventory method for inventory valuation and supports gross margin return on investment (GMROI) reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 330 Retail Inventory Method; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS)

description: The quantity of units moved in this transaction. Positive values indicate stock increases (receipts, returns, transfer-in); negative values indicate stock decreases (sales, write-offs, transfer-out). Expressed in the SKU's base unit of measure.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module; GS1 Unit of Measure Standards

description: Standardized reason code providing additional context for the stock movement (e.g., VENDOR_RETURN, DAMAGED_GOODS, CYCLE_COUNT_VARIANCE, THEFT_SHRINKAGE, EXPIRED_PRODUCT, PROMOTIONAL_WRITE_DOWN). Supports shrinkage analysis and loss prevention reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VENDOR_RETURN|DAMAGED_GOODS|CYCLE_COUNT_VARIANCE|THEFT_SHRINKAGE|EXPIRED_PRODUCT|PROMOTIONAL_WRITE_DOWN|CUSTOMER_RETURN|INTER_DC_TRANSFER — promote to reference product]
reference: National Retail Federation (NRF) Loss Prevention Standards; SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module

description: Free-text field for additional context or commentary on this stock movement, entered by warehouse staff, store associates, or system processes (e.g., cycle count discrepancy explanation, damage description, special handling notes). Supports operational investigation and audit.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module

description: The total on-hand inventory quantity for this SKU at this location immediately after this transaction was applied. Represents the running balance of the perpetual inventory ledger and is the authoritative stock position for omnichannel fulfillment visibility.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module

description: The total on-hand inventory quantity for this SKU at this location immediately before this transaction was applied. Enables point-in-time inventory reconstruction and audit trail validation of the perpetual ledger.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module

description: The accounting date on which this stock movement is posted to the general ledger and inventory valuation records. May differ from transaction_timestamp due to cut-off processing, period-end adjustments, or delayed DSD confirmations.
reference: FASB ASC 330 Inventory Accounting; SAP S/4HANA Retail FI/CO Module

description: The source document number from the originating business transaction that triggered this stock movement (e.g., Purchase Order number, Transfer Order number, POS receipt number, Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) number). Enables end-to-end traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Retail MM/SD Modules; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS)

description: The type of source document associated with this stock movement (e.g., PURCHASE_ORDER, TRANSFER_ORDER, POS_RECEIPT, RMA = Return Merchandise Authorization, CYCLE_COUNT, WRITE_OFF_ORDER). Enables document-level drill-through for audit and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Retail MM/SD Modules; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS)

description: Indicates whether this stock ledger entry is a reversal (counter-entry) of a previously posted transaction. True = this record reverses a prior movement. Used to identify corrective entries and maintain a clean audit trail for financial reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Retail FI/CO Module; FASB ASC 330 Inventory Accounting

description: Indicates whether this stock movement was captured or validated using Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology. True = RFID-confirmed movement. Supports inventory accuracy benchmarking and RFID program performance reporting.
reference: GS1 RFID Standards (EPC/RFID); Manhattan Associates WMS

description: The unique serial number of the specific inventory unit involved in this transaction. Applicable for high-value electronics, appliances, and serialized items. Supports specific identification costing, warranty tracking, and CPSC recall management.
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC); SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module; GS1 GTIN Standards

description: Indicates whether this stock movement represents inventory shrinkage (loss due to theft, damage, administrative error, or vendor fraud). True = shrinkage event. Used for shrinkage monitoring, loss prevention analytics, and CPSC/NRF compliance reporting.
reference: National Retail Federation (NRF) Loss Prevention Standards; Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC)

description: Identifies the operational system of record that originated this stock ledger transaction (e.g., SAP_S4 = SAP S/4HANA Retail, ORMS = Oracle Retail Merchandising System, MANHATTAN_WMS = Manhattan Associates WMS, SFCC = Salesforce Commerce Cloud, SAP_CAR = SAP Customer Activity Repository). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: Informatica MDM; ISO 27001 Information Security Management

description: Externally-known business identifier for this stock movement transaction, sourced from the originating system (e.g., SAP material document number, ORMS transaction reference). Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit tracing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS)

description: Current lifecycle state of the stock ledger transaction. POSTED = confirmed and applied to on-hand balance; PENDING = awaiting confirmation; REVERSED = corrected by a counter-entry; VOIDED = cancelled before posting; ERROR = failed validation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Retail FI/CO Module; FASB ASC 330 Inventory Accounting

description: The precise real-world date and time when the stock movement event occurred (e.g., POS scan time, receiving dock timestamp, cycle count completion time). This is the principal business event time, distinct from system audit timestamps.
reference: SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR); SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module

description: Classification of the stock movement event driving this ledger entry. RECEIPT = inbound from supplier or DC; SALE = Point of Sale (POS) depletion; TRANSFER_IN/OUT = inter-location movement; ADJUSTMENT = cycle count correction; RETURN = customer or Return to Vendor (RTV); WRITE_OFF = shrinkage or dead stock removal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RECEIPT|SALE|TRANSFER_IN|TRANSFER_OUT|ADJUSTMENT|RETURN|WRITE_OFF|DSD_RECEIPT|MARKDOWN_WRITE_DOWN|CYCLE_COUNT — promote to reference product]
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module; National Retail Federation (NRF)

business_glossary_term: Unit Cost (Cost of Goods Sold - COGS)
description: The per-unit cost (Cost of Goods Sold - COGS) applied to this stock movement at the time of the transaction. Used for inventory valuation under FIFO, Weighted Average Cost (WAC), or retail inventory method. Sourced from the active cost layer in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: FASB ASC 330 Inventory Accounting; SAP S/4HANA Retail FI/CO Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS)

business_glossary_term: Unit Retail Price (Average Unit Retail - AUR)
description: The retail selling price per unit at the time of the transaction. Used for inventory valuation under the retail inventory method and for calculating gross margin. Supports Average Unit Retail (AUR) analytics and sell-through rate calculations.
reference: Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM); FASB ASC 330 Retail Inventory Method

description: The timestamp when this stock ledger record was last modified in the data platform (e.g., due to a reversal posting, status correction, or revaluation). Used for change data capture, audit trail, and incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.
reference: ISO 27001 Information Security Management; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability)

description: The inventory costing method applied to this stock movement for financial valuation purposes. FIFO = First In First Out; WAC = Weighted Average Cost; RETAIL_METHOD = retail inventory method; LIFO = Last In First Out (where permitted); SPECIFIC_ID = specific identification for high-value items.
reference: FASB ASC 330 Inventory Accounting; SAP S/4HANA Retail FI/CO Module

description: Indicates whether this stock movement is part of a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) arrangement, where the supplier is responsible for maintaining stock levels. True = VMI-managed transaction. Impacts replenishment ownership and supplier performance tracking.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); SAP S/4HANA Retail MM Module; National Retail Federation (NRF)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each stock position record representing a SKU-node combination at a point in time. Primary key for the stock_position data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: GS1 Global Data Model; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

Foreign key linking to merchandising.category. Business justification: Inventory turn, GMROI, and weeks-of-supply KPIs are calculated and reported by merchandise category. Category-level stock position aggregation is required for OTB reconciliation and buyer performance

Foreign key linking to supplychain.demand_forecast. Business justification: Stock position ATP calculations and weeks_of_supply metrics directly consume demand_forecast data. Retail planners link stock positions to the active forecast for sell-through validation and available

Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: Department-level inventory visibility is a core retail store operations process: department managers review on-hand, reserved, and ATP quantities for their department. Enables department-level stock p

Reference to the inventory node (store, Distribution Center (DC), Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC), or dark store) where this stock position is held. Supports omnichannel fulfillment visibility across all network nodes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Module; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Foreign key linking to merchandising.merch_plan. Business justification: Actual vs. planned inventory performance: merch_plan sets inventory turn targets and planned beginning/ending inventory amounts. Linking stock_position to merch_plan enables direct GMROI and inventory

Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Promotional inventory allocation reporting: retailers track which campaign drove a stock position build (forward buy, safety stock uplift). Enables sell-through rate and velocity_band analysis per cam

Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Seasonal inventory position reporting: buyers track on-hand inventory by season to manage sell-through rates and markdown timing. Linking stock_position to season enables actual vs. planned seasonal i
Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this stock position is recorded. Links to the product master in the Product Information Management (PIM) system and Informatica MDM golden record.
reference: GS1 GTIN Standard; Informatica MDM; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS)

description: Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Inventory valuation and replenishment calculations require consistent UOM master reference. stock_position tracks on_hand_qty, reserved_qty, safety_stock_qty — all must reference the authoritative UOM

Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: VMI inventory reporting requires direct vendor attribution on stock_position (is_vmi flag present). Vendor-managed inventory reconciliation, settlement, and compliance reporting depend on knowing whic

Foreign key linking to supplychain.warehouse_zone. Business justification: DC stock positions are physically located within warehouse zones. Linking stock_position to warehouse_zone enables zone-level inventory visibility and slotting optimization — a named retail DC slottin

Quantity of the SKU available to commit to new customer orders or fulfillment requests. Calculated as on-hand minus reserved, damaged, and quarantine quantities. Authoritative ATP signal consumed by Salesforce Commerce Cloud for omnichannel order promising (BOPIS, ROPIS, Ship-from-Store).
reference: NRF Omnichannel Standards; Salesforce Commerce Cloud ATP; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: Rolling average quantity of the SKU sold per day at this node, calculated from SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR) POS transaction data. Used to compute Weeks of Supply (WOS), reorder point, and safety stock. Input to Blue Yonder Demand Planning forecasts.
reference: SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR); Blue Yonder Demand Planning; NRF Demand Planning Standards

description: Supplier or manufacturer batch/lot number for the current inventory of this SKU at this node. Required for batch-managed products (food, pharmaceuticals, cosmetics) to support FDA recall traceability, CPSC compliance, and Return to Vendor (RTV) processing.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); CPSC Recall Standards; GS1 Batch/Lot Traceability; SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Management

description: Timestamp when this stock position record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and GDPR/CCPA data lifecycle management requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); CCPA; ISO 27001 Information Security

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost and inventory valuation amounts (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across international retail markets.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; FASB ASC 830 Foreign Currency; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module

description: Quantity of the SKU identified as damaged, defective, or unsellable at the inventory node. Excluded from Available-to-Promise (ATP) calculations. Tracked for shrinkage reporting, Return to Vendor (RTV) processing, and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) write-down.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; CPSC Product Safety Standards; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Expiration or best-before date of the current inventory batch for perishable or date-sensitive SKUs (grocery, pharmacy, cosmetics). Used for FEFO (First Expired First Out) rotation, FDA food safety compliance, and proactive markdown or disposal before expiry.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); CPSC Product Safety Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Management

description: Quantity of the SKU currently in transit from a supplier, Distribution Center (DC), or another node and expected to arrive at this inventory node. Sourced from Blue Yonder Demand Planning and SAP S/4HANA WM transfer orders. Used for future ATP projection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Module; Blue Yonder Demand Planning; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Accounting method used to value this SKU's inventory for financial reporting. FIFO (First In First Out), LIFO (Last In First Out), WAC (Weighted Average Cost), standard_cost, or retail_method. Governed by FASB ASC 330 and reported in financial P&L statements.
reference: FASB ASC 330 Inventory Accounting; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module; ISO 9001

description: Flag indicating whether this SKU at this node is classified as dead stock — unsold, obsolete inventory with no recent sales velocity. True when sell-through rate is zero and weeks of supply exceeds the dead stock threshold. Triggers markdown or clearance actions.
reference: NRF Merchandise Planning Standards; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS)

description: Indicates whether this SKU at this node is tracked using RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) technology for real-time inventory visibility. True enables automated cycle counts and reduces manual scanning overhead. Relevant for apparel and high-value electronics categories.
reference: GS1 RFID Standards (EPC/RFID); NRF RFID Adoption Guidelines; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Indicates whether this SKU-node stock position is managed under a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) agreement, where the supplier is responsible for maintaining stock levels. When true, replenishment is supplier-initiated via EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) rather than retailer-triggered.
reference: GS1 EDI Standards; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); NRF Supplier Collaboration Standards

description: Date of the most recent manual or system-generated inventory adjustment (positive or negative) for this SKU at this node. Adjustments include shrinkage write-offs, cycle count corrections, and damage write-downs posted in SAP S/4HANA MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; FASB ASC 330 Inventory Accounting; NRF Inventory Standards

description: Date of the most recent physical cycle count or inventory audit performed for this SKU at this node. Used to assess on-hand quantity accuracy, identify shrinkage, and schedule next count. Supports OSHA and internal audit compliance for inventory accuracy.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA MM Module; OSHA Workplace Safety Standards; ISO 9001

description: Date of the most recent goods receipt (inbound delivery) that updated the on-hand quantity for this SKU at this node. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM goods receipt postings and Manhattan Associates WMS inbound transactions. Used for freshness and lead time analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; Manhattan Associates WMS; GS1 EDI 856 ASN Standard

description: Date of the most recent Point of Sale (POS) or e-commerce sale transaction that depleted stock for this SKU at this node. Sourced from SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR). Used for dead stock identification, velocity scoring, and markdown trigger analysis.
reference: SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR); NRF POS Data Standards

description: Maximum allowable on-hand quantity for this SKU at this node, used to prevent overstock and optimize shelf/storage capacity. Defined in Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) and used by replenishment planning to cap order quantities within Open-to-Buy (OTB) constraints.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); Blue Yonder Demand Planning; NRF Assortment Planning Standards

description: Total physical quantity of the SKU currently present at the inventory node, including all stock categories (sellable, reserved, damaged). Sourced from Manhattan Associates WMS goods receipt and adjustment transactions. Core metric for inventory visibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; Manhattan Associates WMS; NRF Inventory Standards

description: Quantity of the SKU on open purchase orders or replenishment orders placed with suppliers but not yet shipped. Sourced from Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) purchase order lines. Represents future supply commitment within the Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); SAP S/4HANA MM Module; NRF Procurement Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the stock position record. Active: normal sellable stock; frozen: temporarily locked pending investigation; quarantine: damaged or suspect stock held for quality review; blocked: SAP-blocked stock not available for sale; closed: position deactivated (zero stock, node closed).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: The precise date and time at which this stock position snapshot was captured or last updated. Represents the real-world event time of the inventory state. Critical for real-time ATP (Available-to-Promise) accuracy and omnichannel fulfillment SLA compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; Manhattan Associates WMS; NRF Real-Time Inventory Standards

description: Quantity of the SKU placed in quarantine pending quality inspection, regulatory hold, or recall investigation. Separate from damaged stock. Relevant for FDA food safety holds, CPSC recall compliance, and supplier quality disputes.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act; CPSC Recall Standards; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Minimum on-hand quantity threshold at which a replenishment order is automatically triggered for this SKU at this node. Calculated by Blue Yonder Demand Planning based on lead time, demand velocity, and safety stock parameters. Core input to replenishment trigger logic.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning; SAP S/4HANA MM MRP Module; NRF Replenishment Standards

description: Current replenishment workflow status for this SKU-node combination. normal: no action needed; triggered: reorder point breached, order pending; in_progress: replenishment order placed and open; suspended: replenishment paused (e.g., seasonal end, discontinuation); vmi: under Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) control.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); SAP S/4HANA MM MRP Module

description: Quantity of the SKU reserved against confirmed customer orders, BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), ROPIS (Reserve Online Pick Up In Store), or internal transfer orders. Not available for new ATP commitments until reservation is released or fulfilled.
