Predicted total revenue value this customer will generate over their entire relationship with the business, calculated using RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary) models and predictive analytics.
Timestamp when the customer profile record was first created in the master data management system.
Classification of customer as individual consumer (B2C) or corporate buyer (B2B), including special categories such as employee or VIP.
Date of birth of the customer, used for age verification, lifecycle marketing, and personalized birthday promotions.
Primary email address for customer communication across all channels including e-commerce, loyalty programs, and promotional campaigns.
Boolean flag indicating customer consent to receive promotional emails and newsletters.
Boolean flag indicating whether the customer is an employee eligible for employee discount programs.
Legal first name or given name of the customer as recorded in the master data management system.
Complete legal name of the customer, typically concatenated from first, middle, and last name components.

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Consent Date
description: Timestamp when GDPR consent was granted by the customer, required for compliance audit trails.

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Consent Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has provided explicit consent for data processing under GDPR requirements.
Self-identified gender of the customer, used for personalized merchandising and assortment planning.
Date of the customer's most recent interaction with the business across any touchpoint including POS, e-commerce, mobile app, or customer service.
Timestamp of the most recent update to any attribute in the customer profile record.
Legal last name or family name of the customer as recorded in the master data management system.
Date of the customer's most recent purchase transaction across all channels, used for recency analysis in RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary) segmentation.

Current stage in the customer lifecycle journey, used for targeted marketing campaigns and retention strategies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospect|new|active|at_risk|dormant|churned|reactivated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Current tier level in the loyalty program, determining benefits, discounts, and personalized services.
Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has consented to receive marketing communications including email, SMS, and direct mail.

business_glossary_term: Master Data Management (MDM) Golden Record Confidence Score
description: Confidence score (0-100) from Informatica MDM indicating the quality and reliability of the golden record match and data completeness.

business_glossary_term: Master Data Management (MDM) Last Match Date
description: Timestamp of the last MDM matching and survivorship process execution that updated this golden record.
Name of the primary source system that contributed the authoritative data for this golden record (e.g., SAP CAR, Salesforce Commerce Cloud, POS).
Middle name or initial of the customer. Nullable field for customers without a middle name.
Mobile phone number used for SMS notifications, mobile app authentication, and last-mile delivery coordination.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the customer's nationality or citizenship.
Most recent Net Promoter Score indicating customer satisfaction and likelihood to recommend, scored from -100 to 100.
Primary contact phone number for customer service, order notifications, and BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store) communications.
Customer's preferred method for receiving communications and notifications from the business.
ISO 639-2 three-letter language code representing the customer's preferred language for communication and marketing materials.
Current operational status of the customer profile, controlling access to services and transactions across all channels.
Boolean flag indicating customer consent to receive promotional SMS messages and mobile notifications.
Boolean flag indicating whether the customer is designated as a VIP for special clienteling services and exclusive offers.

Unique surrogate identifier for each digital catalog record representing a channel-specific published view of a SKU on a given storefront. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a digital presentation asset managed and published via Salesforce Commerce Cloud.

Foreign key linking to merchandising.assortment_item. Business justification: In retail, a digital catalog listing is authorized by an assortment_item record that approved the SKU for the online channel. This link enables assortment sell-through reporting by channel, lifecycle

Foreign key linking to product.product_brand. Business justification: Ecommerce brand pages, brand-filtered search, and brand-level promotions require catalog entries to reference the authoritative brand record. brand_name is a denormalized string; a proper FK enables b

Foreign key linking to merchandising.buyer. Business justification: Buyers manage online assortment, pricing strategy, and product content decisions for their assigned categories. Digital catalog accountability and performance reporting require buyer attribution for m

Identifier of the primary digital storefront category (navigation taxonomy node) under which this SKU is merchandised online. May differ from the master merchandise hierarchy in ORMS to reflect digital-specific category management and assortment breadth strategies.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Category Management; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS)

description: Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: Drop-ship and delivery promise: digital_catalog.is_drop_ship_eligible requires knowing which DC fulfills the item to display accurate lead times and shipping promises online. Role-prefix 'fulfilling_'

Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_node. Business justification: Online catalog displays store-level availability for BOPIS and ship-from-store fulfillment options. Enables "available at nearby stores" feature and store inventory lookup for omnichannel shoppers.

Foreign key linking to product.item_hierarchy. Business justification: Ecommerce catalog navigation, faceted browsing, and category-level reporting depend on product hierarchy. Retail domain experts expect digital catalog entries to reference the product taxonomy node di

Foreign key linking to product.image. Business justification: Ecommerce catalog listings must reference the approved product image record for display. Linking digital_catalog to product.image enforces referential integrity, enables image lifecycle management (ex

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Catalog items are assigned to profit centers for gross margin analysis, markdown accounting, channel profitability reporting, and product line P&L in retail management accounting systems.

Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Online catalog organized by retail seasons (Spring/Summer, Fall/Winter, Holiday). Seasonal merchandising drives publish/unpublish schedules, promotional calendars, and assortment planning. Critical fo

Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Catalog publishing process requires authoritative link to product master for attributes, compliance flags, lifecycle status, vendor data, and pricing rules. Retail operations depend on single source o

Foreign key linking to pricing.sku_price. Business justification: Digital catalog product pages must display authoritative online prices from sku_price, which maintains channel-specific and zone-specific pricing. Core business process: e-commerce price display and c

Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Segment-targeted catalog publication: retail merchandising teams publish catalogs for specific customer segments (VIP members-only catalog, B2B wholesale catalog). Segment-level catalog visibility con

Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor_contract. Business justification: Exclusive/private-label catalog authorization: vendor contracts with exclusivity_flag or private-label terms govern which products can be listed online. Merchandising compliance requires linking a cat

Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor_item. Business justification: Drop-ship catalog management: a digital catalog listing for a drop-ship product must reference the specific vendor_item record to surface accurate lead times, pack configuration, country of origin, an

The canonical URL of the product detail page (PDP) used to prevent duplicate content issues across multiple storefront URLs or faceted navigation paths. Declared in the HTML link rel=canonical tag. Managed in Salesforce Commerce Cloud.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — SEO Configuration; Google Search Central Guidelines

description: Percentage score (0.00–100.00) measuring how complete the digital content is for this catalog record, based on presence of required fields such as title, description, images, SEO metadata, and rich media. Used to prioritize content enrichment efforts and improve Conversion Rate (CR).
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Content Quality Scoring; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: The date and time when this digital catalog record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and content age analysis. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.
reference: ISO 27001 — Information Security Audit; GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity and Accountability

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the online_price for this digital catalog record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency storefronts in international e-commerce operations.

Indicates whether at least one product video (e.g., demonstration, unboxing, lifestyle) is published for this SKU on the storefront. Rich media presence is a key driver of Conversion Rate (CR) improvement in digital commerce.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Rich Media Management; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: Total number of product images published for this SKU on the storefront, including hero, alternate angles, lifestyle, and swatch images. Used to measure digital content completeness and identify SKUs requiring additional imagery.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Digital Asset Management; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: Indicates whether this SKU is fulfilled via Drop Ship, where the vendor ships directly to the customer without passing through a Distribution Center (DC). Relevant for extended aisle and marketplace assortment strategies.
reference: NRF — Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) and Drop Ship Standards

description: Indicates whether this SKU has been manually designated as a featured product by digital merchandisers, qualifying it for placement in featured product carousels, homepage spotlights, or category hero positions on the storefront.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Merchandising Rules; NRF — Visual Merchandising Standards

description: Indicates whether the SKU is currently available for purchase on the digital storefront. Distinct from publication_status — a product may be published but temporarily unavailable due to inventory depletion or fulfillment constraints. Drives add-to-cart eligibility.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Inventory Availability; NRF Omnichannel Standards

description: Indicates whether this SKU is a private label (store brand) product owned and sold exclusively by the retailer. Private label products typically carry higher gross margins and are a key strategic differentiator in assortment planning.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) — Assortment Planning; NRF — Private Label Standards

description: Indicates whether this SKU is indexed and returned in on-site search results on the digital storefront. A product may be published but excluded from search (e.g., exclusive landing page products or clearance items managed via direct URL only).
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Search Index Configuration

description: The exact date and time when this digital catalog record was most recently published or re-published to the live storefront. Used for content freshness tracking, cache invalidation, and audit trails in Salesforce Commerce Cloud.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Publish History; ISO 27001 — Audit Trail Requirements

description: IETF BCP 47 locale code specifying the language and regional variant for this digital catalog record (e.g., en-US, fr-CA, es-MX). Supports multi-locale storefront content management and international e-commerce operations in Salesforce Commerce Cloud.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Localization; IETF BCP 47 — Language Tags

description: Full consumer-facing product description displayed on the product detail page (PDP), including features, benefits, materials, and usage instructions. Supports rich HTML formatting. Managed in Salesforce Commerce Cloud content management.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Product Detail Page (PDP); FDA — Food Labeling Requirements (for grocery SKUs)

description: The consumer-facing product title as displayed on the digital storefront. May differ from the master product name in PIM to reflect channel-specific merchandising copy or A/B tested titles. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE IDENTITY_LABEL category.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Product Detail Page (PDP); NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the digital catalog record on the storefront. Controls whether the SKU is visible and purchasable by online shoppers. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|published|unpublished|scheduled|archived|suspended — promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Catalog Management; NRF Omnichannel Standards

description: The date on which this digital catalog record is scheduled to be or was deactivated and removed from the storefront. Used for seasonal assortment management, limited-time offers, and clearance markdown lifecycle management.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Catalog Scheduling; Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM)

description: The date on which this digital catalog record is scheduled to become or became active and visible on the storefront. Used for pre-launch product scheduling and promotional launch coordination in Salesforce Commerce Cloud.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Catalog Scheduling; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: Average customer review rating for this SKU on the digital storefront, on a scale of 0.00 to 5.00. Aggregated from verified purchase reviews. Used in product ranking algorithms, content completeness scoring, and conversion rate optimization analytics.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Ratings and Reviews; NRF — Customer Experience Standards

description: Total number of published customer reviews for this SKU on the digital storefront. Used alongside rating_average to assess review volume credibility and identify SKUs requiring review generation campaigns.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Ratings and Reviews; FTC — Endorsement and Testimonial Guidelines

description: The HTML meta description tag for the product detail page (PDP), summarizing the product for search engine result snippets. Typically 150-160 characters. Drives organic Click-Through Rate (CTR) from search engine results pages.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — SEO Configuration; Google Search Central Guidelines

description: The HTML page title tag for the product detail page (PDP), optimized for search engine indexing. Typically limited to 60 characters per SEO best practices. Managed in Salesforce Commerce Cloud content management.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — SEO Configuration; Google Search Central Guidelines

description: Brief consumer-facing product description displayed in product listing pages (PLP) and search results. Typically 100-200 characters. Sourced from Salesforce Commerce Cloud product content and may differ from the master description in PIM.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Product Information Management (PIM) Integration; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: Numeric position used to control the default display sequence of this SKU within its assigned digital category on the storefront. Lower values appear first. Used by merchandisers to manually curate product listing page (PLP) rankings.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Merchandising Rules; NRF — Visual Merchandising Standards

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this digital catalog record originated or was last updated. Supports data lineage tracking across Salesforce Commerce Cloud (SFCC), Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS), Informatica MDM, and Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM).
reference: Informatica MDM — Master Data Management; ISO 27001 — Data Lineage

description: Tax classification code assigned to this SKU for digital commerce tax calculation purposes, determining applicable sales tax rates at checkout. Mapped to tax engine rules in Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Relevant for FDA-regulated food items with tax exemptions.
reference: FASB ASC 606 — Revenue Recognition; FDA — Food Tax Exemption Classifications; FTC — Pricing Transparency

description: The date and time when this digital catalog record was most recently modified, including content updates, price changes, status transitions, or SEO edits. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 27001 — Information Security Audit; GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity and Accountability

description: The human-readable URL path segment for the product detail page (PDP), used in constructing the storefront URL. Must be lowercase, hyphen-separated, and unique per storefront. Example: 'mens-slim-fit-chino-pants-navy'.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — URL Management; Google Search Central Guidelines

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each digital storefront or online channel record. Primary key for the storefront master data product within the e-commerce domain.
reference: Informatica MDM — Master Data Management golden record identifier convention

description: Foreign key linking to merchandising.assortment_plan. Business justification: In retail, each ecommerce storefront (e.g., .com, mobile app, marketplace) is governed by a specific assortment plan defining SKU breadth, depth, and channel strategy. This link enables channel-level

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each e-commerce storefront incurs operating costs (hosting, platform fees, marketing) tracked against a cost center for opex budgeting and P&L reporting. Retail finance teams allocate channel costs to

Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: Order routing and SLA management: each ecommerce storefront (BOPIS, ship-to-home, ship-from-store) is served by a primary DC facility. Retail operations teams use this link to configure fulfillment ro

Identifier of the product catalog assigned to this storefront within Salesforce Commerce Cloud or the PIM system. Determines which assortment of SKUs, categories, and product content is surfaced to customers on this channel. Aligns with Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) assortment planning and Informatica MDM product golden records.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Catalog Management; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); GS1 — Product Identification Standards

description: Foreign key linking to store.format. Business justification: Omnichannel assortment and pricing configuration: a storefront inherits format-level assortment breadth, pricing strategy type, and fulfillment capability flags (BOPIS, SFS, ROPIS). Retail operators c

Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_node. Business justification: Storefront configuration defines primary fulfillment node for ship-from-store and BOPIS operations. Business process: storefront fulfillment network assignment, default inventory source for online ord

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Multi-country retail storefronts post revenue to a designated accounting ledger (e.g., IFRS vs local GAAP). Financial close and revenue recognition reporting require knowing which ledger governs each

Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: Storefronts often represent specific physical stores' online presence for store-specific inventory visibility, local pricing, BOPIS fulfillment, and "shop my store" features. Real business process: st

Identifier of the price book or pricing list assigned to this storefront, sourced from Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM) or Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Governs the base retail prices (AUR — Average Unit Retail) and EDLP (Everyday Low Price) rules applied to products displayed on this channel. Critical for channel-specific pricing strategy execution.
reference: Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM); NRF — Pricing Strategy Standards; FASB ASC 606 — Revenue Recognition

description: Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: A storefront operates within a defined price zone that governs which prices are displayed to online shoppers. Zone-based ecommerce pricing (regional competitive pricing, currency zones) requires store

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_node. Business justification: BOPIS, ROPIS, and ship-from-store operations require each storefront to have a primary fulfillment node assignment. Retail operations teams use this link for node capacity planning, SLA configuration

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Each storefront (site, channel, locale) maps to a profit center for P&L reporting, segment analysis, management accounting, and channel profitability tracking in retail finance systems.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.loyalty_program. Business justification: Storefront has is_loyalty_enabled and ecommerce_integration_enabled exists on loyalty_program, but no FK ties a storefront to its operating loyalty program. Retail storefronts must reference their loy

Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_calendar. Business justification: Each ecommerce storefront operates under a governing promotional calendar that drives event scheduling, blackout periods, and planning lock dates. Retail digital operations teams assign a promo_calend

Foreign key linking to returns.return_policy. Business justification: Each e-commerce storefront channel has a distinct return policy (return_policy.sales_channel confirms this). Channel-specific return policy configuration is a standard retail operational requirement —

Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Retail storefronts are configured per selling season (holiday, back-to-school, spring). The season drives the storefront's active catalog window, promotional calendar, and OTB alignment. A retail merc

Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor_contract. Business justification: Marketplace/vendor storefront governance: in retail marketplace models, a vendor-operated storefront is authorized and governed by a vendor contract (covering terms, exclusivity, payment). Operations

The web analytics platform tracking identifier assigned to this storefront (e.g., Google Analytics 4 Measurement ID 'G-XXXXXXXXXX' or Adobe Analytics Report Suite ID). Used to attribute digital customer journey events, CTR (Click-Through Rate), CR (Conversion Rate), and session data to the correct storefront channel. Classified confidential as it represents proprietary analytics configuration.
reference: GDPR Article 5 — Data Minimisation; CCPA — Consumer Data Tracking Disclosure; ISO 27001 — Information Security

business_glossary_term: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Opt-Out Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether this storefront has activated the CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' opt-out mechanism, required for storefronts serving California consumers. Governs data sharing controls and consumer rights workflows.
reference: CCPA — California Consumer Privacy Act (Cal. Civ. Code §1798.100); CPRA — California Privacy Rights Act

description: Classification of the digital commerce channel operated by this storefront. Segments storefronts by their primary consumer interface: web (desktop/browser), mobile_app (iOS/Android native), marketplace (third-party platform such as Amazon or eBay), social_commerce (shoppable social media), or dark_store (fulfillment-only digital channel). Drives channel-specific analytics including CTR, CR, and GMV reporting.
reference: NRF — Omnichannel Commerce Framework; Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Channel Type taxonomy

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code indicating the primary geographic market served by this storefront (e.g., 'USA', 'CAN', 'GBR'). Used for regulatory compliance scoping (GDPR, CCPA, FTC), tax jurisdiction assignment, and regional assortment planning.
reference: ISO 3166-1 — Country Codes; GDPR Article 3 — Territorial Scope; CCPA — California Consumer Privacy Act

description: The timestamp when this storefront record was first created in the system of record (Salesforce Commerce Cloud or Informatica MDM). Represents the audit trail creation event for data governance and lineage tracking purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: ISO 27001 — Audit Logging Requirements; GDPR Article 5 — Accountability Principle; Informatica MDM

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the primary transactional currency for this storefront (e.g., 'USD', 'CAD', 'EUR'). Governs pricing display, checkout totals, AOV calculations, and GMV reporting. Aligns with Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM) currency configuration.
reference: ISO 4217 — Currency Codes; FASB ASC 606 — Revenue Recognition; Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM)

description: The operational customer service email address displayed to customers on this storefront for support inquiries, returns (RMA — Return Merchandise Authorization), and complaints. Routed to Salesforce Service Cloud for case management. Classified confidential as an organizational contact identifier.
reference: GDPR Article 13 — Information to be Provided; FTC — Consumer Protection Contact Requirements; Salesforce Service Cloud

description: The calendar date on which this storefront was or is scheduled to be permanently retired and removed from active digital commerce operations. Nullable for currently active storefronts. Used for channel sunset planning, data retention scheduling, and regulatory compliance archival.
reference: ISO 27001 — Information Lifecycle Management; GDPR Article 17 — Right to Erasure

description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code representing the default display language for this storefront (e.g., 'en', 'fr', 'es'). Used when a customer's browser language preference cannot be resolved or when the storefront operates in a single-language mode. Distinct from locale_code which also encodes regional formatting.
reference: ISO 639-1 — Language Codes; W3C Internationalization Standards

description: The primary public-facing domain URL for this storefront (e.g., 'https://www.retail.com' or 'https://m.retail.com'). Used for SEO configuration, SSL certificate management, CDN routing, and digital marketing attribution. Classified confidential as it represents proprietary digital infrastructure configuration.
reference: ISO 27001 — Information Security Management; PCI DSS — Secure URL and domain management

description: The minimum order value (in the storefront's primary currency) at which free standard shipping is automatically applied at checkout. A key lever for AOV (Average Order Value) optimization and customer acquisition. Configured in Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM) or Salesforce Commerce Cloud promotion rules.
reference: Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM); NRF — Promotions Management Standards; FTC — Advertising and Pricing Transparency

description: Indicates whether this storefront is subject to GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) consent requirements, triggering cookie consent banners, data processing notices, and opt-in workflows for customers in EU/EEA jurisdictions. Drives compliance configuration in Salesforce Commerce Cloud and CRM systems.
reference: GDPR Article 7 — Conditions for Consent; GDPR Article 13 — Information to be Provided; EU ePrivacy Directive

business_glossary_term: Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether this storefront supports the BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store) fulfillment mode, allowing customers to purchase online and collect from a physical store location. Drives fulfillment routing logic in the OMS (Order Management System) and store operations workflows.
reference: NRF — Omnichannel Fulfillment Standards; Salesforce Commerce Cloud — BOPIS Configuration

description: Indicates whether this storefront supports drop-ship fulfillment, where vendors ship products directly to customers without passing through Retail's DC (Distribution Center). Relevant for extended assortment and marketplace-style operations. Aligns with supplier management and EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) integration.
reference: NRF — Drop Ship Vendor Standards; GS1 — EDI Standards for Direct-to-Consumer Fulfillment

description: Indicates whether this storefront is integrated with Retail's customer loyalty and engagement program, enabling points accrual, redemption, and CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) tracking at the digital channel level. Drives loyalty-specific promotional targeting and RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary) segmentation.
reference: NRF — Customer Loyalty Program Standards; Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Loyalty Integration

business_glossary_term: Reserve Online Pick Up In Store (ROPIS) Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether this storefront supports ROPIS (Reserve Online Pick Up In Store), allowing customers to reserve items online without payment and collect them in-store. Distinct from BOPIS in that no payment transaction occurs at the digital checkout stage.
reference: NRF — Omnichannel Commerce Standards; Salesforce Commerce Cloud — ROPIS Configuration

description: Indicates whether this storefront supports SFS (Ship-from-Store) fulfillment, where physical store locations act as fulfillment centers for online orders. Enables distributed inventory utilization and faster last-mile delivery. Integrates with Manhattan Associates WMS for store-level pick/pack/ship operations.
reference: NRF — Ship-from-Store Operational Standards; Manhattan Associates WMS — Store Fulfillment Module

description: Indicates whether this storefront supports ship-to-home fulfillment, where orders are dispatched from a DC (Distribution Center) or store directly to the customer's delivery address. Governs last-mile delivery eligibility and carrier integration activation for this channel.
reference: NRF — Last Mile Delivery Standards; Manhattan Associates WMS — Fulfillment Mode Configuration

description: Indicates whether product prices displayed on this storefront include taxes (tax-inclusive, common in EU/UK markets) or exclude taxes (tax-exclusive, common in US markets). Governs price display logic at the product listing and checkout stages. Aligns with Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM) tax display configuration.
reference: FASB ASC 606 — Revenue Recognition; Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM); EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC

description: The timestamp when this storefront record was most recently updated in the system of record. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: ISO 27001 — Audit Logging Requirements; GDPR Article 5 — Accountability Principle; Informatica MDM

description: The calendar date on which this storefront was first made publicly available to customers. Used for channel age analysis, anniversary promotions, and historical performance benchmarking. Represents the effective-from date for the storefront's commercial lifecycle.
reference: NRF — Digital Commerce Channel Lifecycle Management

description: IETF BCP 47 locale code defining the primary language and regional formatting for this storefront (e.g., 'en_US', 'fr_CA', 'es_MX'). Controls language display, date/number formatting, and content localization within Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Critical for omnichannel customer experience consistency.
reference: IETF BCP 47 — Language Tag Standard; ISO 639-1 Language Codes; ISO 3166-1 Country Codes

description: The maximum number of distinct line items (SKUs) permitted in a single shopping cart session on this storefront. Governs cart capacity limits configured in Salesforce Commerce Cloud to manage checkout performance and order processing capacity. Relevant for B2B or bulk-purchase storefronts.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Cart Configuration; NRF — E-Commerce Operations Standards

description: The minimum cart value (in the storefront's primary currency) required for a customer to complete checkout on this storefront. Used to enforce MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) value thresholds, qualify for free shipping promotions, or meet marketplace seller requirements. Configured in Salesforce Commerce Cloud checkout rules.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Checkout Configuration; NRF — Pricing and Promotions Standards

description: The PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard) merchant compliance level assigned to this storefront, determined by annual card transaction volume (Level 1: >6M transactions; Level 2: 1M–6M; Level 3: 20K–1M; Level 4: <20K). Governs the scope of annual security assessments and SAQ (Self-Assessment Questionnaire) requirements. Classified confidential as it reveals security posture.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 — Merchant Compliance Levels; PCI SSC — Merchant Level Definitions

description: The version identifier of the Salesforce Commerce Cloud (SFCC) platform release deployed for this storefront (e.g., 'SFCC 23.7', 'B2C Commerce 22.10'). Used for release management, compatibility tracking, and upgrade planning across the digital commerce estate.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Release Management Documentation

description: The default robots meta directive applied to this storefront, controlling whether search engine crawlers may index pages and follow links (e.g., 'index_follow' for the main site, 'noindex_nofollow' for staging or dark-store channels). Impacts organic search visibility and SEO strategy.
reference: NRF — Digital Commerce SEO Standards; FTC — Digital Advertising Transparency

description: The default HTML meta title tag content for the storefront's homepage, used by search engines for indexing and SERP (Search Engine Results Page) display. Impacts organic search traffic and digital marketing performance. Managed within Salesforce Commerce Cloud site preferences.
reference: NRF — Digital Commerce SEO Best Practices; FTC — Advertising Standards for Digital Channels

description: The number of minutes of customer inactivity after which a shopping session is automatically expired on this storefront. Governs cart abandonment timing, session-based analytics, and security compliance for authenticated customer sessions. Configured in Salesforce Commerce Cloud site security settings.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 — Session Management Requirements; ISO 27001 — Access Control; Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Security Configuration

description: The externally-known unique site identifier assigned within Salesforce Commerce Cloud (SFCC) for this storefront. Used as the operational reference key across digital commerce platform configurations, API calls, and integration touchpoints. Aligns to the SFCC Site object's siteID field.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Site Management API; GS1 digital channel identification standards

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this storefront record was sourced or last updated (e.g., 'SFCC' for Salesforce Commerce Cloud, 'MDM' for Informatica MDM, 'MANUAL' for manually entered records). Used for data lineage tracking, reconciliation, and master data governance within the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: Informatica MDM — Data Lineage Standards; ISO 27001 — Information Asset Management

description: Human-readable display name of the digital storefront or online channel (e.g., 'Retail Main Site', 'Retail Mobile App', 'Retail Marketplace — Amazon'). Used in reporting dashboards, operational tooling, and business communications.
reference: NRF — Digital Commerce Channel Naming Standards

description: Current operational lifecycle state of the storefront. 'active' indicates the storefront is live and accepting customer traffic; 'maintenance' indicates a temporary service window; 'draft' indicates pre-launch configuration; 'decommissioned' indicates the channel has been permanently retired. Governs whether the storefront is eligible for traffic routing, promotional targeting, and digital commerce operations.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Site Status management; NRF Digital Operations Standards

description: Comma-delimited list of payment methods accepted at checkout on this storefront (e.g., 'credit_card,debit_card,paypal,apple_pay,google_pay,gift_card,buy_now_pay_later'). Governs payment gateway configuration and PCI DSS compliance scope for this channel. Managed within Salesforce Commerce Cloud payment processor settings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|paypal|apple_pay|google_pay|gift_card|buy_now_pay_later|bank_transfer — promote to reference product]
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 — Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard; PCI SSC — Accepted Payment Methods; Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Payment Integration

description: Code identifying the primary tax jurisdiction applicable to this storefront for sales tax, VAT, or GST calculation purposes (e.g., 'US-CA', 'CA-ON', 'GB'). Used by the tax engine integrated with Salesforce Commerce Cloud to apply correct tax rates at checkout. Aligns with FASB ASC 606 revenue recognition and FTC pricing transparency requirements.
reference: FASB ASC 606 — Revenue Recognition; FTC — Pricing Transparency Requirements; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO — Tax Configuration

description: Identifier referencing the visual theme or template applied to this storefront within Salesforce Commerce Cloud (e.g., 'sfra-retail-v3', 'pwa-kit-2024'). Controls the storefront's UI/UX presentation layer, including layout, typography, and brand styling. Used by digital merchandising and visual merchandising teams for planogram-equivalent digital shelf configuration.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Storefront Reference Architecture (SFRA) Documentation

description: IANA time zone identifier for the storefront's primary operating time zone (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'America/Los_Angeles'). Used for scheduling promotional campaigns, flash sales, maintenance windows, and business-hours-based SLA calculations. Aligns with Salesforce Commerce Cloud site time zone configuration.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; NRF — Digital Commerce Operations Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each individual visitor session on the digital storefront. Primary key for the web_session data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.
Reference to the shopping cart created or interacted with during this session. Enables abandoned cart analysis and cart-level GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) attribution even when no order is placed.

Reference to the order placed during this session, if is_order_placed is True. Enables direct linkage between digital session analytics and order-level GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) and AOV (Average Order Value) reporting.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Order Management; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.loyalty_membership. Business justification: When a loyalty member logs into the ecommerce storefront, the session must be linked to their membership to drive personalized catalog display, member-tier pricing, and targeted offer presentation. Re
Reference to the authenticated customer associated with this session. Null for anonymous/guest sessions. Enables online-to-offline attribution and CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) analysis.

Reference to the loyalty program membership associated with this session, if the visitor is an authenticated loyalty member. Supports Customer Loyalty and Engagement Program analytics and CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) attribution.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Loyalty Integration; NRF Customer Loyalty Standards

description: Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Sessions originate from promotional emails, paid search for promotional keywords, or circular ad QR codes; tracking campaign attribution at session level is fundamental to digital marketing ROI, omnic

Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_offer. Business justification: Session-level offer attribution and digital marketing ROI reporting require web sessions to reference the specific promo_offer that drove the visit. Retail digital teams track offer-driven session con
Reference to the digital storefront or e-commerce site where the session occurred. Supports multi-brand and multi-region digital commerce operations.
Name of the web browser used during the session (e.g., Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge). Used for compatibility analysis and identifying browser-specific conversion drop-offs in the digital funnel.
Version string of the web browser used during the session. Supports technical compatibility analysis and identification of outdated browser populations impacting checkout CR (Conversion Rate).

Version identifier of the consent notice or cookie policy presented to the visitor at session start. Enables compliance audit trails demonstrating which consent framework version was in effect at the time of data collection.

Audit timestamp indicating when this web session record was first captured and written to the Silver Layer data product. Distinct from session_start_timestamp which reflects the real-world event time.

Indicates whether the visitor provided explicit consent for data collection and tracking during this session, as required under GDPR and CCPA. True = consent granted. Governs permissible use of session data for analytics and personalization.
Category of device used by the visitor during the session. Supports omnichannel digital journey analytics and device-specific CR (Conversion Rate) and AOV (Average Order Value) reporting.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Device Detection; NRF Omnichannel Standards

description: Full URL of the last page viewed before the visitor ended the session. Used to identify drop-off points in the digital funnel and optimize checkout flow to improve CR (Conversion Rate).

Fulfillment method selected or browsed during the session. BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), ROPIS (Reserve Online Pick Up In Store), home_delivery, ship_from_store (SFS), or drop_ship. Supports omnichannel attribution and last-mile delivery analytics.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Fulfillment Options; NRF Omnichannel Standards

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code inferred from the visitor's IP address or browser locale. Supports international digital commerce analytics and regional GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) reporting.

IP address of the visitor at session initiation. Used for geolocation inference, fraud detection, and bot traffic filtering. May be considered PII under GDPR/CCPA in some jurisdictions. Stored in compliance with data minimization principles.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140; PCI DSS Requirement 1

description: Indicates whether the session was identified as automated bot or crawler traffic rather than a genuine human visitor. True = bot session. Used to filter non-human traffic from CR (Conversion Rate), CTR (Click-Through Rate), and Footfall analytics.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Bot Detection; FTC Advertising Standards

description: Indicates whether the session was a bounce — the visitor viewed only a single page and exited without further interaction. True = bounce session. Used to calculate bounce rate as a digital storefront health KPI.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Session Analytics; NRF Digital Commerce KPIs

description: Indicates whether the visitor created a shopping cart (added at least one item) during this session. True = cart was created. Key funnel stage flag for CR (Conversion Rate) and abandoned cart analysis in Salesforce Commerce Cloud.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Management; NRF Digital Commerce KPIs

description: Indicates whether the visitor initiated the checkout flow during this session. True = checkout was started. Enables measurement of cart-to-checkout conversion rate and identification of checkout abandonment in the digital funnel.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Checkout Flow; NRF Digital Commerce KPIs

description: Indicates whether the session resulted in a completed order placement. True = order was placed. Primary conversion flag for CR (Conversion Rate) calculation and GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) attribution to digital sessions.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Order Management; NRF Digital Commerce KPIs; FASB ASC 606 Revenue Recognition

description: Full URL of the first page viewed by the visitor upon entering the digital storefront. Critical for measuring landing page CR (Conversion Rate) and identifying high-performing entry points in the digital customer journey.
The most recent on-site search term entered by the visitor during the session. Supports merchandising and assortment planning by revealing demand signals and zero-result search patterns.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Search Analytics; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS)

description: Operating system of the visitor's device during the session (e.g., Windows, macOS, iOS, Android). Used for device ecosystem analysis and platform-specific performance optimization.
Total number of distinct pages viewed by the visitor during the session. Indicator of session engagement depth and product discovery behavior. Used in basket analysis and digital journey analytics.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Session Analytics; NRF Digital Commerce KPIs

description: High-level channel classification of how the visitor arrived at the digital storefront. Supports digital marketing attribution, CTR (Click-Through Rate) analysis, and channel-level CR (Conversion Rate) reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: organic_search|paid_search|social|email|direct|affiliate|display|referral|sms|push_notification — promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Marketing Attribution; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: Full URL of the referring page or external site that directed the visitor to the digital storefront. Null for direct traffic. Enables granular attribution analysis beyond high-level referral_source classification.

Number of on-site search queries executed by the visitor during the session. Indicator of product discovery intent and search effectiveness. Used to optimize PIM (Product Information Management) and assortment depth.

Total elapsed time in seconds between session_start_timestamp and session_end_timestamp. Raw measurement used to compute average session duration KPIs and correlate engagement with CR (Conversion Rate) and AOV (Average Order Value).
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Session Analytics; NRF Digital Commerce KPIs

description: The precise date and time when the visitor session ended, either through explicit logout, timeout, or browser close. Null if session is still active. Used to compute session duration.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Session Management; ISO 8601

description: The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the visitor session was initiated on the digital storefront. Principal business event timestamp for session lifecycle analysis.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Session Management; ISO 8601

description: Current lifecycle state of the web session. 'active' = session in progress; 'completed' = session ended with a conversion event; 'abandoned' = session ended without conversion; 'timed_out' = session expired due to inactivity; 'bounced' = single-page session with immediate exit.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Session Management; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: Externally-known alphanumeric session identifier generated by Salesforce Commerce Cloud (SFCC) at session initiation. Used for cross-system session correlation and digital journey stitching across touchpoints.
Audit timestamp indicating when this web session record was last modified in the Silver Layer, such as when session_end_timestamp or conversion flags are updated upon session close.

Urchin Tracking Module (UTM) campaign parameter identifying the specific marketing campaign name or promotion (e.g., spring_sale_2024, loyalty_reactivation). Links sessions to Pricing and Promotions Management campaigns.
reference: Google Analytics UTM Parameter Standards; Salesforce Commerce Cloud Marketing Attribution

description: Urchin Tracking Module (UTM) content parameter used to differentiate ads or links pointing to the same URL (e.g., banner_top, text_link). Supports A/B testing and creative-level CTR (Click-Through Rate) analysis.

Urchin Tracking Module (UTM) medium parameter identifying the marketing medium (e.g., cpc, email, social, banner). Used alongside utm_source for multi-channel attribution and GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) contribution analysis.
reference: Google Analytics UTM Parameter Standards; Salesforce Commerce Cloud Marketing Attribution

description: Urchin Tracking Module (UTM) source parameter from the session entry URL, identifying the advertiser, site, publication, or other source (e.g., google, newsletter, facebook). Core dimension for digital marketing campaign attribution.
reference: Google Analytics UTM Parameter Standards; Salesforce Commerce Cloud Marketing Attribution

description: Urchin Tracking Module (UTM) term parameter capturing the paid search keyword that triggered the session. Used for paid search keyword-level ROI (Return on Investment) and CTR (Click-Through Rate) analysis.

Classification of the visitor for this session. 'new' = first-time visitor; 'returning' = previously visited but not authenticated; 'authenticated' = logged-in registered customer; 'guest' = checkout without account. Supports CR (Conversion Rate) segmentation and personalization.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Visitor Segmentation; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: Unique system-generated identifier for a shopping cart instance created during a customer's digital session on a storefront. Serves as the primary key for the cart data product and is referenced by cart line items, abandoned cart recovery workflows, and order conversion records.

Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: B2B account-based cart management: corporate accounts require credit limit validation and tax-exempt status checks at cart creation. Retail B2B platforms track cart-to-account for approval workflows,

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Internal employee purchases and corporate procurement via ecommerce platforms require cost center attribution for departmental charge-back accounting, budget tracking, and GL posting in retail finance

Foreign key linking to promotion.coupon. Business justification: Coupon validation and abandoned-cart coupon recovery reporting require the cart to reference the coupon entity directly. Retail analysts track coupon-driven cart conversion rates by coupon_id. The exi

Reference to the order record created when this cart was successfully converted at checkout. Null for carts that have not yet converted (active, abandoned, or expired). Enables cart-to-order traceability, Conversion Rate (CR) measurement, and reconciliation between the digital commerce platform and the Order Management System (OMS).
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Order Object; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: Reference to the physical store location selected by the customer for Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) or Reserve Online Pick Up In Store (ROPIS) fulfillment. Null for ship-to-home and drop-ship carts. Supports online-to-offline attribution, store-level BOPIS volume reporting, and inventory reservation at the designated store.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud BOPIS Configuration; NRF Omnichannel Standards

description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.loyalty_membership. Business justification: Cart already tracks loyalty_points_earned and loyalty_points_redeemed but has no FK to the member. Linking cart to loyalty_membership enables real-time points preview, member-specific pricing, and aba

Reference to the authenticated customer who owns this cart. Null for guest/anonymous sessions where no login has occurred. Used for abandoned cart recovery, Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) attribution, and personalization workflows.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Customer Object; GDPR Article 4

description: Reference to the primary promotion applied to this cart. Used for promotion effectiveness measurement, discount attribution, and digital marketing ROI analysis. A cart may have one primary promotion; additional promotions are tracked at the line level.
reference: Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM); Salesforce Commerce Cloud Promotions

description: Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_offer. Business justification: Offer stacking rule enforcement and maximum redemption limit checking at cart level require the cart to reference the specific promo_offer applied. Retail promotions teams validate offer eligibility a

Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Cart-level shipping address pre-selection: customers assign a saved address to the cart for real-time shipping rate calculation, BOPIS eligibility checks, and delivery date estimation before checkout.

Reference to the digital storefront or site context in which this cart was created. Supports multi-brand, multi-region, and multi-locale storefront configurations within Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Enables Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) and Average Order Value (AOV) analysis by storefront.

The date and time when the cart was classified as abandoned, either by session timeout or explicit inactivity threshold breach. Null for non-abandoned carts. Used to calculate time-to-abandonment metrics, trigger abandoned cart recovery email sequences, and measure recovery window effectiveness.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Abandonment; ISO 8601

description: Classified reason code for why the cart was abandoned. 'session_timeout' indicates the session expired; 'inactivity' indicates no user action within the inactivity window; 'explicit_exit' indicates the customer navigated away; 'payment_failure' indicates checkout was attempted but payment was declined; 'out_of_stock' indicates items became unavailable; 'price_change' indicates a price change triggered cart exit. Null for non-abandoned carts. Supports abandonment root cause analysis.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Session Management; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: Human-readable, externally referenceable business identifier for the cart instance. Used in customer service interactions, abandoned cart recovery email communications, and cross-system reconciliation between Salesforce Commerce Cloud and the Order Management System (OMS). Formatted as CART- followed by a 10-digit sequence.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Object; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the shopping cart. 'active' indicates the cart is open and being interacted with; 'abandoned' indicates the session timed out or the customer left without checkout; 'converted' indicates the cart was successfully submitted as an order; 'expired' indicates the cart exceeded the maximum retention window; 'merged' indicates the cart was consolidated with another cart (e.g., guest-to-authenticated merge). Drives abandoned cart recovery workflows and Conversion Rate (CR) reporting.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Lifecycle; NRF Omnichannel Standards

description: The digital channel or interface through which the cart was created and is being managed. Supports omnichannel analytics distinguishing web browser, native mobile application, mobile web browser, email-link-initiated sessions, and Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) kiosk interactions. Critical for channel-level Conversion Rate (CR) and Average Order Value (AOV) analysis.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Channel Configuration; NRF Omnichannel Standards

description: The date and time when the cart was successfully converted to an order at checkout completion. Null for carts that have not converted. Used for cart-to-order conversion time analysis, checkout funnel performance measurement, and Conversion Rate (CR) reporting.

The date and time when the cart instance was first created in the system, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Represents the start of the cart lifecycle. Used for cart age calculation, session duration analysis, and abandonment timeout determination.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Object; ISO 8601

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this cart (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, CAD). Supports multi-currency storefronts and international e-commerce operations. All amount fields on this cart are denominated in this currency.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GS1 Global Standards

description: Indicates whether the customer has provided explicit consent for their cart and behavioral data to be used for personalization, marketing, and analytics purposes (True) or has not consented or opted out (False). Mandatory for GDPR and CCPA compliance. Governs eligibility for abandoned cart recovery communications and behavioral targeting.
reference: GDPR Article 6 Lawful Basis; CCPA Section 1798.120; FTC Privacy Standards

description: Classification of the device category used by the customer to create and interact with this cart. Supports device-level Conversion Rate (CR) analysis, responsive design optimization, and omnichannel customer journey analytics. Derived from user-agent parsing at session creation.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Session Analytics; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: Total monetary value of all discounts applied to the cart, including promotional markdowns, coupon redemptions, and loyalty reward deductions. Represents the aggregate reduction from subtotal_amount. Used for promotion effectiveness measurement and markdown analysis per Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM).
reference: Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM); FASB ASC 606

description: Estimated shipping and handling cost calculated for the cart based on the selected fulfillment type, destination, and applicable shipping promotions. This is an estimate at cart stage; final shipping cost is confirmed at order submission. Relevant for BOPIS and ROPIS carts where this may be zero.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Shipping Calculation; FTC Pricing Transparency

description: Estimated sales tax calculated on the cart based on the customer's shipping destination and applicable tax jurisdiction rules. This is an estimate at cart stage; final tax is confirmed at order submission. Supports pre-checkout transparency requirements per FTC pricing standards.
reference: FTC Pricing Transparency Standards; FASB ASC 606

description: The date and time at which this cart is scheduled to expire and be purged from the active cart pool if not converted or explicitly abandoned. Determined by storefront-level cart retention policy configuration. Used for cart lifecycle management, data retention compliance, and inventory reservation release scheduling.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Expiry Policy; GDPR Article 5 Data Minimization

description: The intended fulfillment method selected by the customer for this cart. Values include standard Ship-to-Home, Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS), Reserve Online Pick Up In Store (ROPIS), Ship-from-Store (SFS), and Drop Ship (vendor direct-to-customer). Drives downstream Order Management System (OMS) routing and Last-Mile Delivery planning.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Fulfillment; NRF Omnichannel Fulfillment Standards

description: IP address of the customer's device at the time of cart creation. Used for geolocation-based tax and shipping estimation, fraud detection, and digital analytics. May be considered PII under GDPR and CCPA in certain jurisdictions. Stored in compliance with data minimization principles.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140; PCI DSS Requirement 6

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this cart has been classified as abandoned based on session timeout or inactivity threshold rules. True when cart_status is 'abandoned'. Drives abandoned cart recovery workflow triggers in the abandoned_cart_recovery product. Supports cart abandonment rate KPI reporting.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Abandonment Rules; NRF E-Commerce Standards

description: Indicates whether this cart was created by an unauthenticated guest user (True) or an authenticated registered customer (False). Guest carts have limited personalization and recovery capabilities. Used for guest-to-registered conversion analysis and Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) attribution.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Guest Checkout; GDPR Article 4

description: Total number of distinct line items (unique Stock Keeping Units / SKUs) currently in the cart. Used in Units Per Transaction (UPT) analysis, basket analysis, and assortment effectiveness reporting. Distinct from total_quantity which counts aggregate units across all line items.
reference: SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR); NRF Basket Analysis Standards

description: The date and time when the cart was most recently updated, including item additions, removals, quantity changes, coupon applications, or address updates. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used to determine cart inactivity duration for abandonment classification and session recency analysis.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Object; ISO 8601

description: IETF BCP 47 locale code representing the language and regional settings of the storefront session in which this cart was created (e.g., en_US, fr_CA, es_MX). Supports multi-locale storefront operations, localized pricing, and regional promotion targeting.
reference: IETF BCP 47 Language Tags; Salesforce Commerce Cloud Localization

description: Estimated number of loyalty reward points the customer will earn upon successful conversion of this cart to an order. Displayed to the customer during checkout to incentivize purchase completion. Actual points are confirmed and awarded at order fulfillment. Supports loyalty program engagement and abandoned cart recovery messaging.
reference: NRF Loyalty Program Standards; Salesforce Commerce Cloud Loyalty Integration

description: Number of customer loyalty reward points applied as a discount on this cart. Supports loyalty program redemption tracking, Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) analysis, and loyalty ROI measurement. Zero if no loyalty points were applied. Integrates with the Customer Loyalty and Engagement Programs business process.
reference: NRF Loyalty Program Standards; Salesforce Commerce Cloud Loyalty Integration

description: Indicates whether an abandoned cart recovery email has been dispatched to the customer for this cart (True) or not yet sent (False). Prevents duplicate recovery communications and supports recovery campaign performance tracking. Integrates with the abandoned_cart_recovery product for full recovery workflow visibility.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Marketing Cloud Integration; GDPR Article 6 Consent; CAN-SPAM Act

description: Number of items the customer has moved from the active cart to a 'saved for later' or wishlist holding area within the same session context. Supports purchase intent analysis, re-engagement campaign targeting, and assortment demand signal capture for merchandise planning.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Wishlist/Save for Later; NRF Merchandise Planning Standards

description: Pre-discount, pre-tax gross merchandise value of all items in the cart at their listed retail prices. Represents the sum of (unit price × quantity) for all line items before any promotions, coupons, or markdowns are applied. Used as the base for Average Order Value (AOV) and Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) calculations.
reference: FASB ASC 606 Revenue Recognition; Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Pricing

description: Estimated grand total for the cart calculated as subtotal_amount minus discount_amount plus estimated_tax_amount plus estimated_shipping_amount. Represents the net amount the customer is expected to pay. Used for Average Order Value (AOV) benchmarking and abandoned cart recovery value prioritization.
reference: FASB ASC 606 Revenue Recognition; Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Totals

description: Aggregate unit count across all line items in the cart (sum of quantities for all SKUs). Supports Units Per Transaction (UPT) calculation and inventory reservation planning. Differs from item_count which counts distinct SKU lines.

Urchin Tracking Module (UTM) campaign parameter captured from the URL at cart creation, identifying the specific marketing campaign that drove the customer session (e.g., summer_sale_2024, loyalty_reactivation). Supports campaign-level Conversion Rate (CR), Average Order Value (AOV), and Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) attribution.
reference: Google Analytics UTM Standards; NRF Digital Marketing Attribution

description: Urchin Tracking Module (UTM) source parameter captured from the URL at cart creation, identifying the marketing channel or platform that drove the customer to the storefront (e.g., google, facebook, email_newsletter). Supports digital marketing attribution, Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) analysis, and Return on Investment (ROI) reporting for paid and organic channels.
reference: Google Analytics UTM Standards; NRF Digital Marketing Attribution

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each line item within a shopping cart. Primary key for the cart_item data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Line Item Object

description: Reference to the parent shopping cart that contains this line item. Establishes the header-to-line relationship between the cart session and its individual items.

Foreign key linking to merchandising.category. Business justification: Cart analytics by merchandise category drive assortment performance reporting, category-level conversion analysis, and merchandising optimization decisions. Replaces denormalized product_category text

Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: Omnichannel department-level sales and shrinkage reporting: cart_item.product_department is a denormalized string. Replacing it with a proper FK to store.department enables department-level online sal

Foreign key linking to ecommerce.digital_catalog. Business justification: A cart item is added from a specific digital catalog listing on a storefront. The digital_catalog record captures the channel-specific published view (canonical URL, SEO title, image, publication stat

Reference to the physical store selected for BOPIS or ROPIS fulfillment of this cart line item. Null for ship-to-home or drop-ship items. Supports online-to-offline attribution and store-level demand signal analysis.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Store Locator; SAP S/4HANA Retail SD Module

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Perishable/batch-tracked e-commerce items (grocery, pharma) require lot-level traceability for food safety recalls and FEFO fulfillment. Retail domain experts managing online grocery or regulated prod

Foreign key linking to pricing.price_override. Business justification: When a cart item price is overridden, the system must reference the authorizing price_override record for audit trail, fraud detection, and customer service dispute resolution. cart_item.price_overrid
Reference to the authenticated customer who owns this cart. Null for guest/anonymous sessions. Used for abandoned cart recovery, Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) analysis, and personalization.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Customer Object; GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140

description: Reference to the item-level promotion applied to this cart line item at the time of add-to-cart. Null if no promotion was applied. Supports Pricing and Promotions Management analytics.
reference: Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM); Salesforce Commerce Cloud Promotion Object

description: Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_offer. Business justification: Line-level offer attribution reporting and discount accuracy auditing require each cart item to reference the specific promo_offer applied. Retail merchandising teams track offer performance at the it

Foreign key linking to inventory.reservation. Business justification: When a customer adds an item to cart, a soft inventory reservation is created per line item to prevent overselling. Retail e-commerce operations require cart_item to reference its reservation for expi
Reference to the master product record associated with this cart line item. Links to the product catalog managed in Informatica MDM and Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS).
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); Informatica MDM Product Golden Record

description: Foreign key linking to pricing.sku_price. Business justification: Cart items resolve pricing at add-to-cart time from sku_price, which provides channel-specific, zone-specific, and promotion-adjusted prices. Core business process: cart pricing resolution and price l

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Cart items validate real-time inventory availability (ATP, on-hand qty) at specific nodes to prevent overselling during add-to-cart and checkout. Critical for omnichannel inventory visibility and BOPI

The web or app session identifier active when this item was added to the cart. Used for digital customer journey tracking, Click-Through Rate (CTR) attribution, and abandoned cart session-level recovery workflows in Salesforce Commerce Cloud.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Session Tracking; GDPR Article 4 (pseudonymous identifier)

description: The digital touchpoint or page from which the customer added this item to the cart. Supports Click-Through Rate (CTR) and Conversion Rate (CR) attribution analysis and product recommendation engine performance measurement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: product_detail_page|search_results|recommendation|wishlist|email_campaign|homepage|category_page|social_media|paid_ad — promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Analytics; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: The date and time when the customer added this item to the shopping cart. Principal business event timestamp for this entity. Used for abandoned cart analysis, session duration analytics, and digital customer journey tracking in Salesforce Commerce Cloud.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Event Timestamp; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: The date and time when this cart line item record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for the Silver Layer record. Distinct from add_to_cart_timestamp which captures the business event time.
reference: ISO 27001 Information Security; Databricks Lakehouse Audit Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values on this cart line item (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, CAD). Supports multi-currency e-commerce operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; PCI DSS v4.0

description: The type of device used by the customer when adding this item to the cart. Supports digital channel analytics, mobile commerce conversion rate (CR) analysis, and omnichannel customer journey attribution.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Session Analytics; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: The monetary discount applied to this line item due to promotions, markdowns, or loyalty rewards. Calculated as list_price minus unit_price multiplied by quantity. Supports promotion ROI and markdown analysis.
reference: Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM); FASB ASC 606

description: The intended fulfillment method selected by the customer for this cart line item. Supports omnichannel fulfillment analytics including Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS), Reserve Online Pick Up In Store (ROPIS), Ship-from-Store (SFS), and Drop Ship operations.
reference: NRF Omnichannel Fulfillment Standards; Salesforce Commerce Cloud Fulfillment Options

description: Optional personalized message entered by the customer for a gift item. Null if is_gift is False or no message was provided. Relevant only when is_gift is True.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Gift Message Feature; GDPR Article 4

description: Indicates whether the customer designated this cart line item as a gift. True if the item is intended as a gift (may include gift wrapping or message); False otherwise. Supports gifting analytics and seasonal demand planning.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Gift Options Feature

description: Indicates whether the product was in stock at the time the item was added to the cart. True if inventory was available; False if the item was added as a pre-order or back-order. Supports stockout-driven cart abandonment analysis.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; Blue Yonder Demand Planning; NRF Inventory Standards

description: Indicates whether the product is a Private Label (store brand) item. True if the product is a store-owned brand; False if it is a national or third-party brand. Supports private label penetration rate and margin analysis.
reference: NRF Private Label Standards; Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS)

description: Current lifecycle status of the cart line item. 'active' indicates the item is in the active cart; 'removed' indicates the customer explicitly removed it; 'saved_for_later' indicates the item was moved to a wishlist/save-for-later list; 'purchased' indicates the item was converted to an order; 'expired' indicates the cart session expired before checkout.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Line Item Status; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: The date and time when this cart line item record was last updated (e.g., quantity change, status change, price update). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 27001 Information Security; Salesforce Commerce Cloud Audit Trail

description: Sequential ordering number of this line item within the parent cart. Used to maintain consistent display order and support basket analysis sequencing.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Line Item Sequence

description: The total monetary value of this cart line item calculated as unit_price multiplied by quantity before tax. Contributes to Average Order Value (AOV) and Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) calculations at the cart level.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Line Item Subtotal; FASB ASC 606 Revenue Recognition

description: The number of loyalty program points earned by the customer for purchasing this cart line item. Supports Customer Loyalty and Engagement Program analytics and Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) calculations.
reference: NRF Loyalty Program Standards; Salesforce Commerce Cloud Loyalty Integration

description: The date and time when this cart line item was converted to a confirmed order line. Null if the item has not been purchased. Used for cart-to-order conversion rate (CR) analysis and Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) attribution.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Order Conversion Event; FASB ASC 606

description: Number of units of the product added to the cart for this line item. Supports Units Per Transaction (UPT) analysis and inventory reservation checks.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Line Item Quantity; NRF Retail Analytics Standards

description: The inventory quantity available for the SKU at the time the item was added to the cart. Captured as a snapshot from the inventory system. Supports out-of-stock analysis, abandoned cart attribution to stockouts, and Fill Rate reporting.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Inventory Availability; Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: The date and time when the customer explicitly removed this item from the cart. Null if the item has not been removed. Used for abandoned cart item-level recovery workflows and assortment affinity analysis.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Cart Event Timestamp; NRF Digital Commerce Standards

description: The date and time when the customer moved this item from the active cart to the saved-for-later list. Null if the item was never saved. Supports wishlist conversion rate analysis and re-engagement campaign targeting.

The operational system of record from which this cart line item record originated. Primarily Salesforce Commerce Cloud for digital storefronts. Supports data lineage tracking and Master Data Management (MDM) reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Informatica MDM Data Lineage Standards; ISO 27001

description: The calculated tax amount applicable to this cart line item based on the product tax category and customer shipping destination. Supports financial reporting and FASB ASC 606 revenue recognition compliance.
reference: FASB ASC 606 Revenue Recognition; FTC Consumer Protection; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Tax Module

description: The applicable tax rate (as a decimal fraction, e.g., 0.0875 for 8.75%) applied to this cart line item. Varies by product category and jurisdiction. Supports tax compliance reporting and financial reconciliation.
reference: FASB ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Tax Configuration

description: Unique system-generated identifier for a checkout session. Primary key for the checkout data product. Tracks the full funnel progression from cart review through order placement.

Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: B2B account-based checkout: corporate accounts have credit limits, tax-exempt status, and net-terms that must be validated at checkout. Retail B2B operations require checkout-to-account linkage for cr

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.carrier_service. Business justification: Checkout shipping method selection directly maps to a carrier_service record. Retail uses this for shipping cost display accuracy, SLA promise validation, and carrier performance reporting by checkout
Reference to the shopping cart that initiated this checkout session. Links the checkout funnel back to the cart and its line items in the e-commerce domain.
