Classification of the substitution relationship. Equivalent indicates same quality and price point. Upgrade indicates higher quality or price. Downgrade indicates lower quality or price. Alternative indicates different but acceptable replacement. Applicable only when relationship_type is substitution.

The dimension along which the variant differs from the parent item. Applicable only when relationship_type is variant. Examples: size for apparel, flavor for beverages, scent for personal care, pack configuration for multi-packs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: size|color|flavor|scent|pack_configuration|material|style|fit|length|width — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The specific value of the variant dimension for the target item. Examples: Small, Medium, Large for size; Red, Blue, Green for color; Vanilla, Chocolate for flavor; 6-pack, 12-pack for pack configuration.
The 13-digit EAN barcode assigned to the target variant item. Used internationally for product identification and scanning. Common in European and global retail operations.

A code that groups related variants together under a common parent style or product family. Used to aggregate sales, inventory, and performance metrics across all variants of a base item. Supports category management and assortment planning.
The Global Trade Item Number assigned to the target variant item. GTIN is the umbrella term for UPC, EAN, and other GS1 product identifiers. Used for global supply chain and EDI transactions.
The 12-digit UPC barcode assigned to the target variant item. Used for point-of-sale scanning and inventory tracking. Each variant SKU typically has its own unique UPC.
Unique identifier for the Stock Keeping Unit. Primary key for the SKU master record.

Identifier of the primary supplier or vendor who provides this SKU to the retailer. Links to the supplier master data.
Indicates whether this SKU has age restrictions for purchase (e.g., alcohol, tobacco, mature-rated products). True if age-restricted, False if not.

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the product was manufactured or produced. Required for customs and regulatory compliance.
Date and time when this SKU record was first created in the master data system. Follows format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Cubic volume of the SKU package calculated as length × width × height. Used for warehouse space planning and transportation optimization. Measured in cubic feet or cubic meters.
Unit of measure for length, width, and height fields. IN (Inch), CM (Centimeter), FT (Foot), M (Meter).
Date when this SKU will be or was discontinued and removed from the active assortment. Null if the SKU is not planned for discontinuation. Follows format yyyy-MM-dd.
13-digit European Article Number used for product identification globally. International barcode standard.
Date when this SKU becomes active and available for sale. Used for new product launches and seasonal assortment planning. Follows format yyyy-MM-dd.
Total weight of the SKU including packaging, measured in the unit specified by weight_unit_of_measure. Used for shipping and logistics calculations.
Global Trade Item Number that uniquely identifies trade items worldwide. Can be 8, 12, 13, or 14 digits. Umbrella term encompassing UPC, EAN, and other GS1 identifiers.
Indicates whether this SKU is classified as hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, and transportation procedures. True if hazmat, False if not.
Height dimension of the SKU package, measured in the unit specified by dimension_unit_of_measure. Used for space planning and logistics.
Number of layers (tiers) that can be stacked on a pallet. Part of the Ti-Hi pallet configuration used in warehouse and distribution center operations.
URL or file path to the primary product image. Used for e-commerce, mobile apps, and digital signage.

Number of consumer units contained in a single sellable pack. For example, a 6-pack of soda has pack_size = 6.
Number of days the product remains sellable and safe for consumption from the date of manufacture or receipt. Critical for perishable goods and inventory rotation.
Abbreviated product description for use in constrained display contexts such as receipts, labels, and mobile interfaces. Typically 40-60 characters.
Detailed textual description of the SKU including key product attributes, features, and specifications. Used for product information management and customer-facing displays.
Indicates whether this SKU can be safely stacked during storage and transportation. True if stackable, False if not.
Storage and transportation temperature requirement for the SKU. AMBIENT (room temperature), REFRIGERATED (32-40°F), FROZEN (below 0°F), CONTROLLED (specific temperature range).
Number of units per layer on a pallet. Part of the Ti-Hi pallet configuration used in warehouse and distribution center operations.
12-digit Universal Product Code used for point-of-sale scanning in North America. Standard barcode identifier for retail products.
The supplier's own product identifier for this SKU. Used for purchase orders and vendor communication.
Volume or capacity of the product, measured in the unit specified by volume_unit_of_measure. Relevant for liquid and bulk products.
Unit of measure for volume field. GAL (Gallon), LTR (Liter), ML (Milliliter), OZ (Fluid Ounce), QT (Quart).
Unit of measure for gross_weight and net_weight fields. LB (Pound), KG (Kilogram), OZ (Ounce), G (Gram).
Width dimension of the SKU package, measured in the unit specified by dimension_unit_of_measure. Used for space planning and logistics.
Unique identifier for the unit of measure. Primary key for the UOM reference master.
Self-referencing FK on uom (base_uom_id)
Numeric multiplier to convert this unit of measure to the base unit. For example, if 1 case = 12 each, the conversion factor is 12. A value of 1 indicates this is the base unit.
Timestamp when this unit of measure record was first created in the master data system. Part of audit trail for data governance and compliance.
Numeric score (0-100) representing the completeness, accuracy, and consistency of this unit of measure record based on Master Data Management (MDM) data quality rules and validation checks.
Date after which this unit of measure is no longer valid for new transactions. Null indicates no planned end date. Part of temporal validity tracking.
Date from which this unit of measure becomes valid and available for use across retail systems. Part of temporal validity tracking.
Standardized GS1 unit of measure code used for Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) and global supply chain interoperability. Maps internal UOM codes to GS1 standards.
Reciprocal of the conversion factor, used for reverse conversions from base unit to this unit. Calculated as 1 divided by conversion_factor.
Boolean flag indicating whether this is the base unit of measure in its class (True) or a derived/alternate unit (False). Base units have a conversion factor of 1.
Boolean flag indicating whether this unit of measure represents a consumer-facing selling unit (True for each, single item, False for case, pallet, or bulk units).

Boolean flag indicating whether fractional quantities are permitted for this unit of measure (True for weight/volume units like pounds or liters, False for discrete count units like cases or pallets).

Boolean flag indicating whether inventory quantities are tracked and managed in this unit of measure (True if used in Warehouse Management System (WMS) and inventory systems, False if conversion-only).
Boolean flag indicating whether this unit of measure can be used for purchase order creation and supplier ordering (True if valid for procurement, False if display-only or internal-use).

Boolean flag indicating whether this unit of measure is used for variable-weight or variable-measure items (True for items sold by weight or volume with varying quantities, False for fixed-count items).
International Organization for Standardization (ISO) unit code per ISO 80000 and ISO 31 standards, ensuring global consistency in measurement representation.
Timestamp when this unit of measure record was most recently updated. Part of audit trail for data governance and change tracking.

Current lifecycle status of the unit of measure: active (in use), inactive (not currently used but retained for historical data), deprecated (being phased out), or pending (awaiting approval for use).
User ID or name of the data steward or system user who last modified this unit of measure record. Part of audit trail for accountability and data governance.

Number of decimal places to which quantities in this unit of measure should be rounded for display and calculation purposes (e.g., 0 for whole units like cases, 2 for currency-like precision, 3 for weight measurements).
Numeric value controlling the display sequence of units of measure in user interfaces and reports. Lower values appear first.

Name or code of the source system from which this unit of measure record originated (e.g., Informatica MDM, SAP S/4HANA MM, Oracle Retail Merchandising System). Used for data lineage and integration tracking.

Standard abbreviated symbol for the unit of measure (e.g., kg for kilogram, lb for pound, L for liter, oz for ounce). Used in labels, reports, and user interfaces.
United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UN/ECE) Recommendation 20 unit code, widely used in international trade and customs documentation.
Classification of the unit of measure by measurement type: weight (mass), volume (capacity), count (discrete units), length (distance), area (surface), or time (duration).

Short alphanumeric code representing the unit of measure (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, LB for pound, KG for kilogram). Used as the business identifier across ordering, inventory, pricing, and distribution systems.
Detailed description of the unit of measure, including usage context and business rules for application across retail operations.
Full descriptive name of the unit of measure (e.g., Each, Case, Pound, Kilogram, Liter, Ounce, Pallet).

Unique surrogate identifier for the profit center master record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the profit_center data product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.

business_glossary_term: Annual Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Budget
description: Planned annual Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) budget for this profit center. Used to calculate budgeted gross margin and GMROI targets. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; FASB ASC 606; NRF

description: Planned annual operating expense (OPEX) budget for this profit center, excluding COGS. Includes store labor, occupancy, marketing, and overhead. Used for EBITDA planning and P&L variance reporting. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; FASB ASC 606; NRF Financial Planning

description: Planned annual revenue target for this profit center in the profit center currency. Used for P&L variance analysis, comp sales (SSS) benchmarking, and EBITDA forecasting. Sourced from SAP CO-PCA budget planning. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; FASB ASC 606; NRF Financial Planning

description: Indicates whether this profit center qualifies as a reportable segment under FASB ASC 280 (Segment Reporting) based on the 10% quantitative thresholds for revenue, profit/loss, or assets (True = reportable segment). Drives external financial disclosure requirements.
reference: FASB ASC 280 Segment Reporting; SEC Regulation S-X

description: The fiscal year or fiscal year-period (YYYY or YYYY-MM) for which the current budget is active for this profit center. Used in Open to Buy (OTB) planning, financial forecasting, and variance analysis against actuals.

description: SAP business area code representing a distinct area of operations for which an internal balance sheet and P&L can be drawn up (e.g., 'RETAIL', 'WHOLESALE', 'ECOM'). Supports cross-company-code reporting and segment disclosures.

Retail channel classification for this profit center indicating how revenue is generated: 'physical_store' (brick-and-mortar), 'ecommerce' (digital), 'omnichannel' (BOPIS/ROPIS), 'wholesale', 'marketplace' (third-party platform), 'dark_store' (fulfillment-only). Enables channel-level P&L and comp sales (SSS) analysis.
reference: NRF Omnichannel Standards; SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA

description: SAP FI company code representing the legal entity to which this profit center belongs. Determines the chart of accounts, fiscal year variant, and currency for financial postings. Required for intercompany transfer pricing and legal entity P&L.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; FASB ASC 606; FASB ASC 842

description: SAP Controlling Area code to which this profit center belongs (e.g., 'US01', 'EU01'). The controlling area defines the organizational unit within which cost and profit center accounting is performed. Required for all CO-PCA postings.

SAP CO cost center code linked to this profit center for overhead cost allocation and internal management accounting. Enables COGS and operating expense tracking at the profit center level for EBITDA calculation.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country in which this profit center operates (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR', 'CAN'). Required for multi-national tax reporting, transfer pricing compliance, and GDPR/CCPA data residency.

Timestamp when this profit center master record was first created in the source system (SAP CO-PCA). Supports audit trail, data lineage, and financial close governance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; ISO 27001; GDPR Article 5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the profit center's local management reporting currency (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). All revenue, COGS, gross margin, and EBITDA figures for this profit center are reported in this currency. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category (principal quantitative context).
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; FASB ASC 830 Foreign Currency

description: Date on which this profit center was deactivated or archived. Null if the profit center is currently active. Used for historical P&L analysis, store closure reporting, and comp sales (SSS) exclusion logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; FASB ASC 280; NRF

description: Indicates whether intercompany transactions for this profit center are subject to elimination during consolidated financial reporting (True = elimination required). Used in group consolidation to remove internal P&L and balance sheet effects per FASB ASC 810.
reference: FASB ASC 810 Consolidation; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO

description: SAP fiscal year variant code defining the fiscal calendar for this profit center (e.g., 'K4' for calendar year, 'V3' for April–March). Determines period-end close dates, budget cycles, and financial reporting periods for EBITDA and P&L tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; FASB ASC 606; FASB ASC 842

description: SAP functional area code classifying the business function of this profit center (e.g., 'SALES', 'PRODUCTION', 'ADMIN', 'DISTRIBUTION'). Used for cost-of-sales accounting and P&L by function reporting per IFRS/GAAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; FASB ASC 606; ISO 9001

description: Code of the standard hierarchy node to which this profit center is assigned in SAP CO-PCA. Enables rollup P&L reporting across organizational levels (e.g., store → region → banner → enterprise). Used for segment reporting per ASC 280.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; FASB ASC 280 Segment Reporting

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this profit center master record. Used for change data capture (CDC), MDM synchronization, and audit trail maintenance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; Informatica MDM; ISO 27001

description: Indicates whether this profit center has active lease obligations subject to ASC 842 lease accounting (True = lease obligations exist). Triggers right-of-use (ROU) asset and lease liability recognition in the financial close process for store and DC profit centers.
reference: FASB ASC 842 Lease Accounting; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO

description: Identifies the authoritative source system from which this profit center master record was originated or last mastered (e.g., 'SAP_S4HANA', 'INFORMATICA_MDM'). Supports data lineage, MDM golden record management, and audit traceability.
reference: Informatica MDM; SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; ISO 27001

description: Indicates whether financial postings to this profit center are currently blocked (True = blocked, False = open). Set during financial close periods, audits, or when a profit center is being restructured. Prevents erroneous postings during sensitive periods.

Management accounting classification indicating the primary financial role of the profit center: 'revenue' (generates external revenue), 'cost' (internal cost allocation only), 'investment' (capital-intensive with ROI tracking), 'service' (shared service provider). Drives transfer pricing rules and internal P&L treatment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; FASB ASC 280; NRF Financial Standards

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the profit center within the controlling area, as defined in SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA (e.g., 'PC_STORE_0042', 'PC_ECOM_US'). Used in journal entries, cost allocations, and management reports. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; FASB ASC 280 Segment Reporting

description: Detailed business description of the profit center's purpose, scope, and the business unit it represents. Provides context for financial analysts, auditors, and management reporting consumers.

Numeric level of this profit center within the management accounting hierarchy (e.g., 1 = enterprise, 2 = banner, 3 = region, 4 = store). Used for hierarchical P&L rollup, drill-down reporting, and segment aggregation.

Human-readable name of the profit center (e.g., 'Chicago Flagship Store', 'US E-Commerce Channel', 'Private Label Apparel'). Used in P&L reports, dashboards, and management presentations. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE IDENTITY_LABEL category.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; NRF Financial Reporting Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the profit center master record. 'active' — open for postings; 'inactive' — temporarily closed; 'locked' — blocked for new postings (e.g., during financial close); 'pending' — awaiting activation; 'archived' — historical record, no postings allowed. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.

Classification of the autonomous business unit represented by this profit center. Determines the nature of P&L reporting: 'store' (physical retail location), 'channel' (e-commerce, BOPIS, DSD), 'product_line' (private label, electronics, apparel), 'banner' (hypermarket, discount outlet), 'region' (geographic grouping), 'corporate' (shared services). Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; FASB ASC 280 Segment Reporting; NRF

description: Geographic region code to which this profit center belongs (e.g., 'NORTHEAST', 'MIDWEST', 'WEST_COAST', 'EMEA'). Used for regional P&L rollup, comp sales analysis, and management reporting by geography.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; NRF Regional Reporting

description: Full name or employee ID of the manager accountable for this profit center's financial performance. Used in management reporting, budget approval workflows, and P&L accountability assignments in SAP CO-PCA.

Financial reporting segment assigned to this profit center per FASB ASC 280 (Segment Reporting). Maps profit centers to reportable segments (e.g., 'GROCERY', 'APPAREL', 'ECOMMERCE', 'INTERNATIONAL') for external financial disclosures and SEC filings.
reference: FASB ASC 280 Segment Reporting; SEC Regulation S-X

description: Abbreviated display name for the profit center used in space-constrained reports, POS system displays, and financial dashboards (e.g., 'CHI-FLAGSHIP', 'ECOM-US').

Physical store identifier associated with this profit center when profit_center_type is 'store'. Links to the store master for footfall, planogram, and comp sales (SSS) reporting. Null for non-store profit centers.

description: Indicates whether transfer pricing rules apply to transactions involving this profit center (True = transfer pricing active). Relevant for intercompany transactions between profit centers, particularly for cross-border operations subject to OECD transfer pricing guidelines.

description: Date from which this profit center is valid and open for financial postings in SAP CO-PCA. Defines the start of the profit center's active period for time-dependent master data management and fiscal year planning.

Date until which this profit center is valid for financial postings. After this date, the profit center is locked for new transactions. Null indicates an open-ended validity period. Used for profit center lifecycle management and financial close.
Unique surrogate identifier for each journal entry record in the general ledger. Primary key for the journal_entry data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.

description: Reference to the SAP CO cost center to which the journal entry is assigned for management accounting and internal P&L reporting. Enables cost allocation across store operations, distribution centers, and corporate functions.

description: SAP S/4HANA ledger identifier specifying which accounting ledger the entry is posted to (e.g., '0L' = Leading Ledger for GAAP/FASB, 'N1' = Non-Leading Ledger for IFRS parallel accounting). Supports multi-GAAP reporting requirements.

SAP S/4HANA four-character company code identifying the legal entity or organizational unit for which the journal entry is posted. Aligns with the FI-GL company code configuration and supports intercompany elimination and entity-level P&L reporting.

Indicates whether the journal entry header represents a debit (D) or credit (C) posting at the document level. Fundamental double-entry bookkeeping indicator used in SAP FI-GL for balance validation and financial statement preparation.

description: The date of the original source document (invoice, receipt, contract, etc.) that triggered the journal entry. May differ from the posting date and is used for aging analysis, vendor/customer reconciliation, and audit trail purposes.

SAP FI accounting document number uniquely identifying the journal entry within a company code and fiscal year. This is the externally-known business identifier used in audit trails, reconciliation, and cross-system references (e.g., EDI, ERP).

description: SAP FI functional area code classifying the journal entry by business function for cost-of-sales P&L reporting (e.g., COGS, Selling, Administration, Distribution, Marketing). Supports income statement presentation under FASB ASC 606.

description: Total amount of the journal entry translated into the company code's local (functional) currency using the FX rate at posting date. Used for statutory P&L, balance sheet, and EBITDA reporting under FASB standards.

description: SAP FI two-digit posting key controlling the debit/credit assignment, account type, and field selection for the journal entry (e.g., 40=Debit G/L, 50=Credit G/L, 01=Customer Invoice, 31=Vendor Invoice). Fundamental SAP FI-GL configuration element.

Current lifecycle state of the journal entry in the SAP FI-GL workflow. 'draft' = entry in preparation; 'posted' = fully posted to ledger; 'parked' = saved but not yet posted (pending approval); 'held' = temporarily saved; 'reversed' = entry has been reversed; 'error' = posting failed validation.

The date on which SAP S/4HANA is scheduled to automatically reverse this journal entry (typically the first day of the following period for accrual entries). Populated when accrual_indicator is True. Supports automated period-end close and accrual management.

description: The transaction amount translated to the company code local currency, corresponding to SAP BSEG-DMBTR. Used for statutory financial reporting and local GAAP compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (DMBTR); FASB ASC 606; GAAP

description: Free-form assignment field used for line item sorting and allocation, corresponding to SAP BSEG-ZUONR. Typically populated with a reference number (e.g., purchase order, cost object) to facilitate reconciliation and reporting.

SAP business area code (GSBER) representing an organizational unit for which an internal balance sheet and P&L can be created. Used for segment reporting across store formats (hypermarket, e-commerce, discount).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (GSBER); FASB ASC 606

description: The date on which this open item was cleared (AUGDT in SAP BSEG). Null for uncleared items. Used for days-payable-outstanding (DPO) and days-sales-outstanding (DSO) calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (AUGDT); FASB ASC 606

description: SAP clearing document number (AUGBL) assigned when this open item is cleared against a payment or offsetting entry. Null for uncleared items. Used in accounts payable/receivable reconciliation and aging analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (AUGBL); FASB ASC 606

description: SAP company code (BUKRS) representing the legal entity for which this journal entry line is posted. Enables multi-entity financial consolidation and statutory reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (BUKRS); FASB ASC 606

description: The timestamp when this journal entry line record was first created in the system, corresponding to SAP BKPF-CPUDT and CPUTM combined. Used for audit trail, financial close monitoring, and SOX compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BKPF (CPUDT/CPUTM); SOX Section 302/404; ISO 27001; GDPR Article 5

description: Indicates whether this line item is a debit (D) or credit (C) posting, corresponding to SAP BSEG-SHKZG. Fundamental to double-entry bookkeeping and trial balance validation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (SHKZG); FASB ASC 606; GAAP double-entry accounting

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the document (transaction) currency, corresponding to SAP BSEG-WAERS. Enables multi-currency P&L and balance sheet reporting.
The date of the originating source document (invoice, receipt, etc.), corresponding to SAP BSEG-BLDAT. May differ from posting date; used for accrual and cutoff analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (BLDAT); FASB ASC 606

description: SAP document type code (e.g., SA=GL posting, KR=vendor invoice, DR=customer invoice, AB=accounting document), corresponding to SAP BKPF-BLART. Classifies the nature of the journal entry for reporting and audit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SA|KR|DR|AB|KZ|DZ|WA|WE|RE|RV — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BKPF (BLART); FASB ASC 606

description: The foreign currency exchange rate applied to translate the document currency amount to local currency, corresponding to SAP BSEG-KURSF. Used for FX gain/loss analysis and multi-currency reconciliation.
Fiscal posting period (1–16, including special periods) within the fiscal year, corresponding to SAP BSEG-MONAT. Drives monthly financial close and period-end reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (MONAT); FASB ASC 606

description: Fiscal year in which this journal entry line is posted, corresponding to SAP BSEG-GJAHR. Used for annual financial close, budgeting, and year-over-year P&L comparisons.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (GJAHR); FASB ASC 606

description: SAP functional area (FKBER) used for cost-of-sales accounting, classifying expenses by business function (e.g., COGS, selling, administration, distribution). Supports P&L by function reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-CO BSEG (FKBER); FASB ASC 606

description: Reference to the SAP internal order (AUFNR) to which this line item is assigned. Used for project-level cost tracking, capital expenditure monitoring, and marketing campaign cost allocation.

Indicates whether this journal entry line has been reversed (True) or is active (False), derived from SAP BKPF-STBLG. Reversed entries must be excluded from financial statement totals to prevent double-counting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BKPF (STBLG); FASB ASC 606; GAAP

description: SAP ledger group (LDGRP) identifying the accounting principle under which this line is posted (e.g., leading ledger for GAAP, non-leading ledger for IFRS). Supports parallel accounting for multi-GAAP reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (LDGRP); FASB ASC 606; FASB ASC 842; IFRS 16

description: Descriptive text for this journal entry line item, corresponding to SAP BSEG-SGTXT. Provides narrative context for auditors, financial analysts, and during financial close review.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (SGTXT); FASB ASC 606

description: Sequential line item number within the parent journal entry, corresponding to SAP BSEG-BUZEI. Used to order and uniquely identify each debit or credit posting within a document.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the company code local currency, corresponding to SAP BSEG-PSWSL. Required for statutory reporting and local GAAP financial statements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (PSWSL); ISO 4217; FASB ASC 606

description: SAP material number (MATNR in BSEG) corresponding to the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) associated with this line item. Populated for inventory valuation, COGS, and goods movement postings. Enables drill-down from GL to product-level cost analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM BSEG (MATNR); GS1; NRF

description: The date on which this line item is posted to the general ledger, corresponding to SAP BSEG-BUDAT. Determines the fiscal period and year for financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (BUDAT); FASB ASC 606

description: Two-digit SAP posting key (BSCHL) that determines the account type, debit/credit indicator, and field selection for this line item. Examples: 40=GL debit, 50=GL credit, 31=vendor credit, 01=customer debit.

The quantity associated with this line item (MENGE in SAP BSEG), applicable for material-related postings such as goods receipts, inventory adjustments, and COGS entries. Enables unit-level cost analysis.

External reference document number (XBLNR from BKPF) such as a vendor invoice number, purchase order number, or customer order number. Enables cross-system reconciliation between ERP and source operational systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BKPF (XBLNR); GS1 EDI standards

description: The SAP document number of the reversing entry (STBLG from BKPF), if this line has been reversed. Enables audit trail tracing from original posting to reversal for financial close and SOX compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BKPF (STBLG); SOX Section 302/404; FASB ASC 606

description: SAP special GL indicator (UMSKZ) used for down payments, guarantees, and other special transactions that are posted to alternative reconciliation accounts. Blank for standard postings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (UMSKZ); FASB ASC 606

description: The tax amount (VAT/sales tax) associated with this line item in document currency, corresponding to SAP BSEG-WMWST. Required for tax compliance reporting and accounts payable/receivable reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (WMWST); FTC; FASB ASC 606

description: Tax code assigned to this line item for VAT/sales tax determination, corresponding to SAP BSEG-MWSKZ. Drives tax reporting, compliance, and accounts payable/receivable tax calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (MWSKZ); FTC; FDA (food tax); ISO 9001

description: Unit of measure for the quantity field (MEINS in SAP BSEG), such as EA (each), KG (kilogram), or CS (case). Required for material-related GL postings to support COGS and inventory valuation analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI BSEG (MEINS); GS1; ISO 80000

description: The effective date for cash flow and bank reconciliation purposes, corresponding to SAP BSEG-VALUT. Used in treasury and accounts payable/receivable aging analysis.

SAP WBS element (PROJK) for project-based cost assignment, corresponding to capital expenditure projects such as store openings, DC expansions, or IT implementations. Supports CAPEX tracking and ASC 842 lease accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-CO BSEG (PROJK); FASB ASC 842

description: Unique identifier for the tax posting transaction record in SAP S/4HANA FI-TX.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Tax Posting Object

Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Customer invoices (AR) generate sales tax and VAT postings in retail operations. Linking tax_posting.ar_invoice_id → ar_invoice.ar_invoice_id enables direct reconciliation of tax collected on customer
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Tax postings have cost_center (STRING) but no FK to cost_center master. This FK enables cost allocation for tax expenses.

Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: Sales and use tax postings by facility location for nexus determination, tax jurisdiction assignment, and compliance reporting. Required for multi-state retail tax compliance and audit defense.

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Tax postings have gl_account_number (STRING) but no FK to gl_account master. This FK enables account validation for tax postings.

Foreign key linking to supplychain.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Cross-border retail inbound shipments trigger import duties, customs taxes, and freight taxes posted directly against the shipment. Tax authorities require shipment-level tax audit trails. tax_posting

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Tax postings in SAP S/4HANA are ledger-specific — tax amounts may differ between leading ledger (GAAP) and extension ledgers (IFRS, management). Linking tax_posting.ledger_id → ledger.ledger_id enable
Unique identifier of the retail store location where the taxable transaction occurred (for POS sales tax postings).

Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Line-level tax compliance: e-commerce and omnichannel orders require per-line tax postings for multi-jurisdiction nexus reporting and ASC 740 compliance. Retail tax teams trace tax postings to origina
Reference to the original tax posting ID that this record reverses (populated only when reversal_flag is true).

Foreign key linking to order.pos_transaction. Business justification: Sales tax compliance: every POS transaction generates tax postings for jurisdiction-level remittance and audit. Tax authorities require transaction-level traceability. A retail tax compliance analyst

Foreign key linking to order.pos_transaction_line. Business justification: POS line-level tax audit: each POS transaction line has its own tax_code and tax_rate; tax postings must trace to the specific line for sales tax audit defense, jurisdiction remittance, and tax-exempt
Unique identifier of the customer for output tax postings on sales transactions.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Tax postings have profit_center (STRING) but no FK to profit_center master. This FK enables P&L allocation for tax expenses.

Unique identifier of the supplier/vendor for input tax postings on purchase transactions.
Cross-reference to supporting documentation for tax audit defense (original invoice, receipt, exemption certificate, tax calculation log).
The business area code for cross-company-code financial reporting and segment analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Business Area Configuration

description: The SAP company code representing the legal entity responsible for this tax posting.
The timestamp when this tax posting record was first created in the SAP S/4HANA FI-TX system.
The externally-known accounting document number from which this tax posting originated (invoice, receipt, credit memo).
Type of source accounting document that generated this tax posting.
Exchange rate used to convert transaction currency to local currency for GL posting.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the company's local reporting currency for financial consolidation.
The timestamp when this tax posting record was last modified (for correction postings or adjustments).
Flag indicating whether the retailer has established tax nexus (physical or economic presence) in this jurisdiction, triggering tax collection obligation.
reference: US Supreme Court South Dakota v. Wayfair (2018)

description: The date when the tax transaction was posted to the general ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI.
Indicates whether this posting is a reversal of a previously posted tax transaction (for corrections or cancellations).
Indicates whether this is a reverse charge VAT transaction where the customer is responsible for self-assessing and remitting VAT.
The calculated tax amount posted to the tax GL account, in transaction currency.
The tax amount converted to local reporting currency for financial statement consolidation.
The name of the government tax authority to which this tax will be remitted (e.g., California Department of Tax and Fee Administration, IRS).
reference: State and Local Tax Authority Registry

description: The SAP tax code that defines the tax type, rate, and GL account assignment for this posting.
The business event date used to determine applicable tax rates and jurisdiction (typically transaction date or delivery date).
Indicates whether this is input tax (paid to suppliers) or output tax (collected from customers).
Indicates whether this transaction qualified for tax exemption (e.g., resale certificate, government entity, non-profit exemption).
The exemption certificate number provided by the customer to support tax-exempt status (resale certificate, government ID, non-profit certificate).
reference: Streamlined Sales Tax (SST) Exemption Certificate Standards

description: City or municipality name for city-level sales tax jurisdictions (applicable in US multi-jurisdiction tax scenarios).
Composite jurisdiction code uniquely identifying the complete tax authority (country-state-county-city combination).
Three-letter ISO country code of the tax jurisdiction where the tax obligation arises.
County or district name for county-level sales tax jurisdictions (applicable in US multi-jurisdiction tax scenarios).
Two-letter state or province code within the country for state-level sales tax (US) or provincial tax (Canada).
The date when the tax liability arises for VAT purposes (typically invoice date or goods delivery date per VAT regulations).
The effective tax rate percentage applied to the taxable base (e.g., 8.25000 for 8.25% sales tax).
reference: State and Local Tax Authority Rate Tables

description: The retailer's tax registration number with the jurisdiction's tax authority (sales tax permit number, VAT registration number, GST number).
The category of tax being posted (sales tax, VAT, use tax, excise duty, withholding tax, customs duty).
The gross transaction amount subject to tax before tax calculation, in transaction currency.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the tax transaction was recorded.
Unique surrogate primary key for the AP invoice record in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse. Assigned by the data platform upon ingestion from SAP S/4HANA FI-AP.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: AP invoices have cost_center_code (STRING) but no FK to cost_center master. This FK enables cost allocation and reporting. The cost_center table is the SSOT for cost center master data in SAP S/4HANA

Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: AP invoices are posted to fiscal periods. The ap_invoice table has fiscal_year (STRING) and fiscal_period (STRING) but no FK to the financial_period master. This FK enables period-level controls (post

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: AP invoices have gl_account_code (STRING) but no FK to gl_account master. This FK enables account validation, financial statement mapping, and ensures postings use valid GL accounts from the chart of

Foreign key linking to supplychain.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Freight and logistics invoices reference specific inbound shipments for accrual matching, payment processing, and freight cost allocation. Standard AP three-way match process in retail supply chain.

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: In SAP S/4HANA FI, every AP invoice posting generates a corresponding GL journal entry (FI document). Linking ap_invoice.journal_entry_id → journal_entry.journal_entry_id establishes the direct sub-le

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: AP invoices in SAP S/4HANA post to a specific ledger (leading ledger for statutory reporting, extension ledgers for IFRS or management accounting). Linking ap_invoice.ledger_id → ledger.ledger_id enab

Foreign key linking to store.location. Business justification: AP invoices for store-specific vendor deliveries (DSD, store supplies, maintenance) must be attributed to the receiving store location for cost allocation and three-way match. Retail AP teams process

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: AP invoices have profit_center_code (STRING) but no FK to profit_center master. This FK enables P&L allocation and profit center reporting.
Reference to the purchase order against which this invoice is matched during three-way match (PO/GR/Invoice) processing in SAP S/4HANA MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; GS1 EDI Standards

description: Foreign key linking to supplychain.receiving_event. Business justification: Retail AP 3-way match (PO + goods receipt + invoice) requires linking ap_invoice directly to the receiving_event that confirmed physical receipt. goods_receipt_number on ap_invoice is a denormalized r

Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor_contract. Business justification: Retail AP three-way match requires invoices to be validated against the governing vendor contract for payment terms, discount eligibility, and compliance. Finance teams link invoices to contracts for
Reference to the vendor (supplier) master record who issued this invoice. Links to the supplier/vendor master in SAP S/4HANA MM and Informatica MDM golden record.
Amount deducted from the invoice payment as a vendor compliance penalty (chargeback) for violations such as late delivery, labeling errors, or EDI non-compliance. Reduces net payment to vendor.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; NRF Vendor Compliance Standards

description: Date on which the open AP invoice item was cleared (settled) in the SAP FI-AP subledger, either by payment, credit memo offset, or manual clearing. Marks the end of the invoice lifecycle.
SAP FI document number of the clearing transaction (AUGBL) that settled this invoice item. Enables full audit trail from invoice receipt through payment clearing for SOX compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; SOX Compliance; ISO 27001

description: SAP company code (BUKRS) representing the legal entity or organizational unit responsible for this AP invoice. Determines the chart of accounts, fiscal year variant, and currency for financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; FASB ASC 606; FASB ASC 842

description: Timestamp when the AP invoice record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA FI-AP) or ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage for period-end close.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; ISO 27001; GDPR Article 5; CCPA

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the invoice is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency AP processing and foreign exchange revaluation in SAP FI.
Cash discount amount available if payment is made by the discount due date. Represents the financial benefit of early payment and is tracked for cash discount capture rate KPI reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; NRF AP Best Practices

description: Last date by which payment must be made to capture the early payment cash discount offered by the vendor (e.g., 2/10 Net 30 — discount expires on day 10). Critical for cash discount capture optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; NRF AP Best Practices

description: Reason code for invoices placed in disputed status during three-way match or AP review. Drives dispute resolution workflow and vendor communication in SAP FI-AP and Salesforce Service Cloud. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: price_discrepancy|quantity_discrepancy|duplicate_invoice|missing_po|quality_issue|chargeback|other — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; Salesforce Service Cloud; NRF AP Best Practices

description: Calculated date by which payment must be made to the vendor to comply with agreed payment terms and avoid late payment penalties. Derived from invoice date plus payment terms net days in SAP FI-AP.
Exchange rate applied to convert the invoice currency to the company code local currency at the time of posting. Used for foreign currency AP revaluation and P&L reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; ISO 4217; FASB ASC 830

description: SAP fiscal posting period (MONAT) within the fiscal year (01–12 for regular periods, 13–16 for special closing periods). Determines the accounting period for AP subledger and GL reconciliation.
SAP fiscal year (GJAHR) in which the invoice is posted. Used for period-end AP close, financial reporting, and year-over-year AP aging and spend analysis.
Total gross invoice amount before any deductions, discounts, or tax adjustments as stated on the vendor invoice. Represents the full obligation prior to netting. Corresponds to WRBTR in SAP FI.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; FASB ASC 606; ISO 4217

description: Date printed on the vendor invoice document. Used as the baseline date for payment terms calculation (e.g., Net 30 from invoice date) and cash discount eligibility. Corresponds to BLDAT in SAP FI.
Externally assigned invoice number as provided by the vendor on the invoice document. Used for vendor reconciliation, EDI matching, and dispute resolution. Corresponds to BELNR field in SAP FI-AP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; GS1 EDI Standards; FASB ASC 606

description: Current workflow state of the AP invoice through the invoice-to-payment cycle. Drives AP aging analysis, payment run eligibility, and period-end close. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|under_review|approved|posted|scheduled|paid|disputed|cancelled — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; FASB ASC 606; NRF AP Best Practices

description: Classification of the invoice document type as defined in SAP FI-AP document type configuration. Determines posting logic, payment terms applicability, and GL account determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|credit_memo|debit_memo|recurring|intercompany|proforma — promote to reference product]

Indicates whether the invoice was received electronically via EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) transmission from the vendor, as opposed to paper or email. EDI invoices (e.g., ANSI X12 810) enable straight-through processing.
reference: GS1 EDI Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP

description: Indicates whether this invoice is part of a recurring payment schedule (e.g., monthly rent, lease payments under ASC 842, or standing service contracts). Recurring invoices are auto-generated in SAP FI-AP.
Net payable amount after applying all applicable discounts, deductions, and chargebacks. Represents the actual cash outflow obligation. Used for cash flow forecasting and AP aging analysis.

Indicator specifying whether and why the invoice is blocked from automatic payment processing. Corresponds to ZLSPR in SAP FI-AP. A value of 'none' means the invoice is eligible for the next payment run.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; SOX Compliance; ISO 27001

description: Actual date on which payment was executed and disbursed to the vendor via the selected payment method (ACH, wire, check). Populated upon successful payment run completion in SAP FI-AP.
SAP FI document number generated for the payment transaction that clears this invoice. Used for bank reconciliation, remittance advice, and audit trail of the payment-to-invoice clearing.

Method by which the invoice will be or was settled (e.g., ACH electronic transfer, wire transfer, check, virtual card, EFT). Corresponds to ZLSCH in SAP FI-AP and determines payment run processing logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; PCI DSS; NRF AP Best Practices

description: SAP payment terms key (ZTERM) defining the agreed payment conditions with the vendor, including net days, discount percentages, and discount periods (e.g., ZB14 = 2% 10 days Net 30). Drives due date and discount date calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; NRF Vendor Management Standards

description: Date on which the invoice was posted to the SAP general ledger and AP subledger. Determines the accounting period for financial reporting and period-end close. Corresponds to BUDAT in SAP FI.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; FASB ASC 606; FASB ASC 842

description: Date the vendor invoice was physically or electronically received by the AP department. Used to measure invoice processing cycle time and SLA compliance for vendor payment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; NRF AP Best Practices

description: Reference number or text included in the payment remittance advice sent to the vendor, enabling the vendor to match the payment to their outstanding invoices. Supports vendor reconciliation and dispute prevention.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; GS1 EDI Standards

description: Internal SAP FI document number (BELNR) assigned upon posting of the invoice in SAP S/4HANA. Used for cross-reference with the general ledger and FI-AP subledger.
Total tax amount (VAT, GST, sales tax) included in or applicable to the invoice as determined by SAP FI tax code configuration. Required for tax compliance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; FTC Pricing Transparency; FDA (where applicable)

description: Result of the three-way matching process comparing the purchase order (PO), goods receipt (GR), and vendor invoice quantities and amounts in SAP FI-AP/MM. Exceptions require AP team resolution before payment approval.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP/MM; NRF Procurement Standards; SOX Compliance

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the AP invoice record, including status changes, payment updates, or dispute resolution actions. Supports change data capture (CDC) and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; ISO 27001; GDPR Article 5; CCPA

description: Masked or tokenized reference to the vendor bank account used for payment disbursement. Stored in compliance with PCI DSS requirements. Full account details are maintained in SAP FI-AP vendor master banking data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; PCI DSS; PCI SSC

Reference to the B2B customer (wholesale account, franchise, or commercial counterparty) to whom this invoice is issued. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE minimum category.

reference: ISO 4217; FASB ASC 830 Foreign Currency; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR

description: Standardized SAP reason code for the customer dispute (e.g., pricing discrepancy, short shipment, quality issue, chargeback). Used for dispute root-cause analysis and vendor chargeback management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; NRF Chargeback Standards

description: Current status of any customer dispute raised against this invoice. Drives collections workflow routing and dunning block management in SAP FI-AR and Salesforce Service Cloud.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Salesforce Service Cloud; NRF

description: Date by which full payment must be received per the agreed payment terms. Drives AR aging bucket classification (current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+ days) and dunning trigger logic.
Indicates whether dunning notices are blocked for this invoice (True = blocked). Set when a dispute is open, a payment arrangement is in place, or the account is under review.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Salesforce Service Cloud

description: Date of the most recent dunning notice sent to the customer for this invoice. Used to track collections activity and enforce dunning interval rules.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; NRF Collections Best Practices

description: Current dunning escalation level (0–4) assigned to this invoice in SAP FI-AR dunning program. Level 0 = no dunning; higher levels indicate escalating collection notices sent to the customer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; NRF Collections Best Practices

description: Fiscal accounting period (01–12 for regular periods, 13–16 for special closing periods) within the fiscal year. Drives period-end AR close, aging bucket assignment, and monthly P&L reporting.
Fiscal year in which the invoice is posted (e.g., 2024). Used for annual P&L reporting, EBITDA tracking, and year-end AR close reconciliation.
Total gross billed amount before tax, discounts, or adjustments. Represents the face value of goods or services invoiced to the B2B customer. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET minimum category.

The business date on which the invoice was issued to the customer. Represents the principal real-world event time for revenue recognition under ASC 606. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP minimum category.
Current lifecycle state of the AR invoice. Drives AR aging analysis, dunning workflow, and period-end close. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS minimum category.

Classification of the invoice document type in SAP FI-AR. Distinguishes standard customer invoices, credit memos for returns/adjustments, debit memos for chargebacks, proforma invoices, and intercompany billing.
Net total amount due after applying discounts and before tax. Represents the contractual obligation for payment. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET net total component.
Remaining unpaid balance on the invoice (net_amount minus applied_amount). Drives AR aging analysis, dunning escalation, and collections workflow prioritization.
Indicates whether the invoice is blocked from automatic payment processing (True = blocked). Applied during dispute resolution, credit hold, or compliance review.
Agreed payment instrument for settling this invoice (e.g., ACH bank transfer, wire, check). Determines cash application routing and bank clearing workflow in SAP FI-AR.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; PCI DSS (PCI SSC)

description: SAP payment terms key (e.g., NT30, NT60, 2/10NET30) defining the payment schedule, early payment discount percentage, and due date calculation rules for this invoice.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; NRF Payment Standards

description: The accounting date on which the invoice is posted to the general ledger in SAP FI-AR. Determines the fiscal period for revenue recognition and AR balance reporting. May differ from invoice_date for period-end accruals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; FASB ASC 606; GAAP

description: External reference number provided by the customer (e.g., purchase order number, contract number) for remittance matching and EDI reconciliation. Stored in SAP FI-AR XBLNR field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; GS1 EDI Standards

description: Date on which revenue from this invoice was recognized under ASC 606 criteria (performance obligation satisfied). May differ from invoice_date for multi-element arrangements or deferred revenue scenarios.

ASC 606 revenue recognition status for this invoice. Indicates whether revenue has been recognized, is deferred pending performance obligation fulfillment, or has been reversed. Critical for FASB-compliant financial reporting.
SAP sales organization (VKORG) responsible for this invoice. Enables P&L reporting and revenue attribution by sales channel, region, or business unit.
Total tax amount applied to the invoice (sales tax, VAT, or applicable jurisdiction tax). Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET adjustment component.
reference: FASB ASC 606; FTC Pricing Transparency; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the AR invoice record in the source system. Supports change data capture and audit compliance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED minimum category.
Amount written off as uncollectible bad debt for this invoice. Used for bad debt expense reporting, allowance for doubtful accounts calculation, and FASB-compliant AR reserve management.
reference: FASB ASC 310; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; GAAP

description: Date on which the invoice balance was written off as bad debt in SAP FI-AR. Null until a write-off is executed. Required for bad debt aging and allowance for doubtful accounts reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 310; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; GAAP

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each budget record in the financial planning system. Primary key for the budget data product in SAP S/4HANA CO module.
Reference to the cost center or profit center responsible for this budget line. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA CO cost center master data for P&L and EBITDA reporting.

Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: DC operating budgets (labor, utilities, equipment) are planned and tracked by facility. Standard retail budgeting process requires facility-level budget allocation and variance reporting.

Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Budget records are tied to specific fiscal periods. The budget table has fiscal_year (INT) and fiscal_period (INT) as separate columns, but no FK to the financial_period master. This FK enables joinin
Reference to the General Ledger (GL) account associated with this budget line item, enabling line-item granularity for financial reporting and FASB-compliant disclosures.
reference: FASB ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: In SAP S/4HANA, budgets are defined within a specific ledger context (leading ledger for GAAP, extension ledgers for IFRS or management reporting). Linking finance_budget.ledger_id → ledger.ledger_id

Foreign key linking to store.location. Business justification: Store-level budgets must link directly to the store location for budget vs. actual variance reporting. Retail finance teams run store-level budget performance reports. finance_budget has cost_center_i
FK to finance.profit_center
The actual financial amount posted to the GL account and cost center for this budget period. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI actuals for budget vs. actual variance analysis.
reference: FASB ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: The date on which this budget record received final approval from the authorized approver. Required for audit trail and financial governance compliance.
reference: FASB Internal Controls; SAP S/4HANA CO Module

description: Current workflow state of the budget record within the financial planning approval cycle. Tracks progression from draft through submission, review, approval, rejection, or period lock. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|locked — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; FASB Internal Controls

description: The name or employee identifier of the financial authority (e.g., CFO, Finance Director, Cost Center Manager) who approved this budget record. Supports approval workflow audit trail.
reference: FASB Internal Controls; SAP S/4HANA CO Module

description: High-level financial classification of the budget line: Capital Expenditure (CAPEX), Operating Expenditure (OPEX), Cost of Goods Sold (COGS), labor costs, or revenue. Drives P&L and EBITDA reporting segmentation.
reference: FASB ASC 606; FASB ASC 842; NRF Financial Planning Standards

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the budget record, used in financial planning communications, approval workflows, and audit trails. Format: BDG-YYYY-NNNNNN.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; NRF Financial Planning Standards

description: The retail channel (store, e-commerce, omnichannel, wholesale, marketplace) for which this budget is planned. Enables channel-level revenue, COGS, and gross margin projections.
Planned Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for the budget period, used to project gross margin and support merchandise planning and Open to Buy (OTB) calculations.
reference: FASB ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA CO Module

description: The amount of the budget that has been formally committed through purchase orders or contracts but not yet expensed. Supports Open to Buy (OTB) integration and encumbrance accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; FASB ASC 842

description: The timestamp when this budget record was first created in the financial planning system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and financial close process tracking.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this budget record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency financial planning and consolidation.

business_glossary_term: Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization (EBITDA) Projection
description: Planned Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization (EBITDA) for the budget period. Key metric for investor financial guidance and enterprise P&L reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 606; NRF Financial Planning Standards

description: The fiscal period (1–13, where 13 is a special closing period) within the fiscal year to which this budget line applies. Supports monthly and period-level budget vs. actual variance analysis in SAP CO.
The fiscal quarter (1–4) within the fiscal year for this budget record. Used for quarterly reforecast cycles, investor financial guidance, and quarterly P&L reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 606; NRF Financial Planning Standards

description: The fiscal year (e.g., 2024) to which this budget record applies. Aligns with the retail fiscal calendar in SAP S/4HANA FI for annual financial planning and investor guidance.
reference: FASB ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: The latest forward-looking financial estimate for this budget line, updated during rolling forecast or reforecast cycles. Used for forecast vs. prior forecast variance analysis and investor guidance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: The methodology used to derive the forecast or budget amounts: top-down (executive-driven allocation), bottom-up (cost center aggregation), driver-based (KPI-linked), statistical (model-driven), or zero-based budgeting.
reference: NRF Financial Planning Standards; Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Planned gross margin (revenue minus COGS) for the budget period and business unit. Supports Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) analysis and financial close reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 606; NRF Financial Planning Standards

description: Indicates whether this budget record has been locked for the period, preventing further edits. True when the period is closed or the budget has been formally locked post-approval.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; FASB Internal Controls

description: Indicates whether this budget record represents a reforecast (True) rather than the original budget plan (False). Distinguishes reforecast iterations from baseline budget entries for variance tracking.
The date on which this budget record was locked to prevent further modifications, typically at period-end close. Supports financial close process integrity and audit compliance.
reference: FASB ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: Free-text field for financial planners to document assumptions, rationale, or commentary associated with this budget record. Supports audit trail and management review during financial planning cycles.
reference: FASB Internal Controls; NRF Financial Planning Standards

description: The Open to Buy (OTB) amount representing the remaining purchasing budget available for merchandise procurement in this period. Calculated as planned inventory budget minus committed and received inventory costs.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS); NRF Merchandise Planning Standards

description: The end date of the planning period covered by this budget record. Defines the effective close of the budget window for financial reporting and period-end close processes.
reference: FASB ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA CO Module

description: The start date of the planning period covered by this budget record. Defines the effective beginning of the budget window for financial close and reporting alignment.
reference: FASB ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA CO Module

description: Classifies the budget record by planning cycle type: original budget (initial annual plan), revised budget (approved amendment), rolling forecast (monthly update), quarterly reforecast, or annual plan. Maps to SAP CO plan version category.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; FASB ASC 606

description: The original planned financial amount for this budget line item in the functional currency. Represents the baseline against which actuals and forecasts are compared in budget vs. actual reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA CO Module

description: The forecast amount from the immediately preceding forecast cycle, retained for forecast vs. prior forecast variance analysis and trend tracking in financial planning.
Forward-looking revenue estimate for the business unit and channel in this budget period. Supports top-line financial planning, investor guidance, and Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) tracking.
reference: FASB ASC 606; NRF Financial Planning Standards

description: The date on which this budget record was formally submitted for approval in the financial planning workflow. Supports submission tracking and planning cycle compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; FASB Internal Controls

description: The name or employee identifier of the person who submitted this budget record for approval. Supports submission tracking and financial planning accountability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; FASB Internal Controls

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this budget record. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.

The absolute difference between planned amount and actual amount (actual_amount minus planned_amount) for this budget line. Positive value indicates overspend; negative indicates underspend. Supports budget vs. actual reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 606; NRF Financial Planning Standards

description: The percentage variance between planned and actual amounts ((actual - planned) / planned * 100). Used in financial dashboards and P&L reporting to flag material variances requiring management action.
reference: FASB ASC 606; NRF Financial Planning Standards
